title: Business Analyst
description: We’re passionate about designing and delivering top-notch digital experiences for our clients — and their customers — and helping them create efficiencies using data and technology. But what’s most important about us is that we have a diverse team of experts all dedicated to getting clients from goals to outcomes — and that’s where you come in. This is a hybrid position. Will need to work 3 days a week in offices located in Columbus, OH. As a Business Analyst, you’ll be joining our team of talented experts. The right candidate will possess the following experience: Responsibilities: Manages ongoing relationship with business partner (Product) to drive satisfaction. Coordinates project delivery by facilitating design reviews and walk-throughs to communicate systems designs and validate proposed solutions. Obtains key inputs from enterprise architecture team and identifies solution interdependencies. Serve as a key liaison to Product and other departments that have specific business functions. Collaborate with multiple Product, Shared Services and Backend Data teams to identify, document and communicate business needs and provide technology solutions. Responsible for eliciting high level requirements and Acceptance Criteria provided by the Product team, creating and refining epics and stories to Dev Ready. Answer questions and clarify requirements for projects and enhancements during the estimation, refinement, development and QA process. Responsible for developing and maintaining requirements traceability and facilitating refinement sessions. Works independently and proactively to meet Refinement deadlines, i.e., ensuring the backlog is always adequately filled with fully refined stories. Creates meeting agendas with team input. Captures, assigns and tracks meeting action items and follow-ups. Establishes and leads agile practices including sprint planning, team stand-ups, retrospectives and design reviews/demos. Creates and maintains the team's sprint plans and backlog in identified system (ex Jira, Azure DevOps) Tracks team capacity through sprint capacity and velocity. Identifies if new skillsets are needed and works with LOB leaders to identify and ramp in new individuals. Reports on project budget and proactively identifies potential impacts to the budget escalating if needed through Change Control processes. Knowledge, Skills & Abilities: Be an integrated member of the agile team Ability to manage multiple, concurrent projects, activities and tasks under time constraints. Be an independent problem solver with the ability to creatively translate business data/requirements into technical solutions. Have outstanding written and verbal communication skills.? Have strong interpersonal skills and the ability to work effectively with a wide variety of technical and non-technical stakeholders Anticipate change and quickly prioritize accordingly. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Excellent facilitation and organizational skills. Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in computer science or related technical discipline. 4+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst or other related field. 8+ years of overall IT experience with significant experience in software development. 2+ years of project management experience Direct experience with client / stakeholder communications Experience leading cross-functional teams Experience planning, facilitating, eliciting, documenting and managing business, functional and technical requirements. Experience with Agile / Lean methodologies. Experience with Microsoft Products; Excel, Word and Visio. Experience with project tools such as JIRA and Confluence (or other Agile tools). Required and Desired Experience/Skills: Budget management experience More about G2O We’re different than other companies at G2O. We blend the research and design, technology, and data expertise to deliver the solutions our clients crave — and we do all of this as one in-house team, from vision to execution. We’re also the largest company of our kind based in Ohio to do this — and have been evolving how we do it for 40 years. Individually, we bring a wealth of experience from diverse backgrounds — personally and in business. We’re a diverse and passionate team of leaders and experts in technology, data, analytics, design, content, and more. But we think we’re best when we put our minds together. Each person brings something distinct to our team — some unique flavor to their background or their experience. That makes for stronger collaboration — and elevates the outcomes for our clients. Are you ready to collaborate to greatness with us? Learn more at G2O.com/careers

title: Business Analyst
description: Job Description Role Summary This is an ONSITE position based at our location in Gastonia, NC. No Visa sponsorships are available for this position. The Business Analyst for Regional Digital Enablement serves as the key link between regional business needs and digital solution delivery. The role ensures that local requirements are accurately captured, assessed, and transformed into concrete digital initiatives that support both regional objectives and global digital strategy. This position plays a central role in evaluating digital opportunities, driving the adoption of digital tools, and ensuring that regional teams can fully leverage technology to improve customer experience, operational efficiency, and commercial performance. This Business Analyst must combine strong business acumen, analytical clarity, and the ability to navigate complex changing environments. Job Responsibilities Collect and structure business requirements from regional stakeholders (sales, marketing, CX, operations) Translate business needs into functional specifications and user stories Conduct gap analyses to assess opportunities for digitalization and process improvement Develop non-strategic or lightweight digital tools (e.g., calculators, dashboards, …) to meet urgent or specific regional requirements Adapt and localize existing global tools to ensure usability and relevance in the regional context Maintain documentation for all regional tools, including user guides and technical specifications Collaborate closely with global Digital teams when regional needs intersect with the strategic tech stack Ensure seamless integration of regional solutions into the broader system landscape Participate in testing cycles (UAT) and secure sign-off before deployment Identify and nurture digital initiatives that are not centrally prioritized but are essential for regional growth or customer satisfaction Evaluate feasibility, impact, cost, and risk for such initiatives Challenge business requirements and proposed tools to ensure alignment with customer experience, commercial goals, and operational efficiency Recommend improvements and alternative solutions when relevant Lead change management activities related to new tools or digital processes Train local sales teams through workshops, webinars, and use-case-driven sessions Monitor adoption and usage patterns; propose corrective actions where needed Requirements Education: Bachelor’s Degree in Business, Information Systems, or a related field Experience: 3 years’ minimum experience in business analysis, digital transformation, or similar roles Previous experience working with CRM systems, digital platforms, and analytics tools Required Skills: Proven track record in designing or adapting localized digital tools Practical experience in developing customer-centric digital initiatives (e.g., customer portals, digital sales aids, dashboards) Fluency in English; additional languages an advantage depending on region Business Acumen: Deep understanding of commercial drivers and regional market dynamics Proficiency using Business Intelligence tools (i.e. Power BI) Analytical Thinking: Ability to interpret data, define KPIs, and derive actionable recommendations Digital Fluency: Understanding of digital tools relevant to CRM, sales enablement, customer portals, data analytics, workflow automation, etc. Change Management: Strong capability in training, onboarding, and supporting adaptation Communication & Collaboration: Able to manage diverse stakeholders and build consensus Preferred Education, Experience, and Skills: Master's Degree Development and coding skills using Python, HTML, DAX, SQL Salesforce CRM experience Previous similar experience in Automotive Aftermarket - Onsite

title: Business Analyst
description: About Koia: Koia makes delicious, plant-based protein shakes that are low in sugar, high in nutrition, and made from real ingredients. We're on a mission to make healthy choices more accessible, convenient, and crave-worthy. As a rapidly growing brand in the functional beverage space, we’re building a team of passionate, driven individuals who thrive in a fast-paced environment. Overview: We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented Business Analyst to join our team. This role will be critical in supporting our leadership and sales teams by delivering insights from retail data platforms such as SPINS and Numerator, maintaining and updating financial models, and managing trade and promotional spend analytics. The Business Analyst will also collaborate closely with the sales team to develop compelling sales presentations for key retail partners, helping to tell a data-driven story that supports growth and partnership goals. The ideal candidate will have strong Excel/Google Sheets expertise, experience working with syndicated data platforms, and a solid understanding of systems integration to ensure seamless data flow across tools and teams. Key Responsibilities: Data Analysis & Insights (SPINS & Numerator): Analyze retail sales and shopper data using SPINS and Numerator platforms. Deliver actionable insights and trends to sales, marketing, and leadership teams. Create dashboards and reports to monitor performance across key retail channels. Support sales strategy and account planning with data-backed recommendations. Sales Presentation Support: Partner with the sales team to create retailer-facing presentations that highlight brand performance, market trends, and promotional effectiveness. Translate complex data into clear, compelling visuals and narratives tailored to retail buyers. Ensure consistency and accuracy across sales materials using up-to-date data from internal and external sources. Financial Modeling & Forecasting: Update and maintain monthly financial models based on actuals, forecasts, and budgets. Collaborate with Finance to align projections with business goals. Analyze variances between forecast and actuals and provide explanations to key stakeholders. Trade & Promo Spend Analytics: Manage and maintain data platforms that track trade and promotional spending. Evaluate ROI of trade and promotional programs and provide recommendations for optimization. Work cross-functionally with Sales, Marketing, and Finance to ensure accuracy and alignment of spend data. Requirements: Technical Skills: Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets (pivot tables, lookups, dynamic dashboards, macros, etc.). Experience with SPINS, Numerator, or similar syndicated data platforms is preferred. Ability to turn data into visual stories using PowerPoint, Google Slides, or other presentation tools. Qualifications: Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Data Analytics, or a related field preferred. Strong communication skills with the ability to present complex data clearly and concisely. Highly organized, proactive, and capable of managing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

title: Business Analyst Specialist
description: Business Analyst Specialist New York, NY, United States Full time Schedule $78,720 - $131,160 Annually* * based on job, location, and schedule Job Description Be part of an amazing story Macy’s is more than just a store. We’re a story. One that’s captured the hearts and minds of America for more than 160 years. A story about innovations and traditions…about inspiring stores and irresistible products…about the excitement of the Macy’s 4th of July Fireworks, and the wonder of the Thanksgiving Day Parade. We’ve been part of memorable moments and milestones for countless customers and colleagues. Those stories are part of what makes this such a special place to work. Job Overview The Business Analyst enables Macys marketing with data and data management solutions. As a member of the marketing/digital team, the Business Analyst acts as a liaison between the business users and the technology teams to advocate marketing data, data marts and systems/applications. This position enables marketing solutions by assuring quality and availability of associated data. The Business Analysts function is detail intensive, requiring the accurate capture and translation of data requirements (both tactical and analytical needs) and validation/verification of the working solution. What You Will Do Participate in end-to-end documentation of data and reporting requirements for marketing systems projects, including: Identify source systems and data required to meet user’s current and potential future needs. Identify source and target mappings. Define and validate transformation logic required to support information requirements. Participate in solution development process to ensure solution addresses all business requirements. Establish and coordinate user acceptance testing and plan. Conduct testing and validation. Deliver end-user training. Present status, milestones and issues to management and other stakeholders related to assigned projects. Develop the process and supporting artifacts to organize information management into data dictionary, data lineage, and other supporting documents. Maintain a thorough understanding of analytics, data repositories, data models and reporting systems, including: Understand and document the underlying data elements, constraints, flows and sources supporting the business domain. SME (subject matter expert) on macys.com business processes, data, marketing/digital eco-system as well as data received from other sources. With understanding of marketing system and data needs, proactively identify gaps in data coverage and identify required enhancements and opportunities for improvement. Work with customers to bring clarity to translate high-level requirements into actionable and achievable products. Help analyze and solve day-to-day challenges with data and analytics/reporting systems, including: Research data and systems issues. Resolve and/or prioritize issue resolution. Proactively identify data quality problems and support solution identification. Interpret highly complex physical and logical data models. Ability to operate as a project coordinator utilizing a cross functional/matrix team. Perform other job-related duties and activities as requested. Skills You Will Need BA/BS Degree, advanced degree preferred or equivalent work experience. 5+ years of business analyst experience. 4+ years working experience with databases. 2+ years SQL. Comfortable using systems/technology including GCP and other cloud platforms . Extremely detail oriented. Strong communication skills and ability to communicate effectively with senior management, architects, data modelers, data visualization experts, and developers. Comfortable working independently to achieve successful results with minimal guidance. Confidently handle multiple projects simultaneously and under deadlines. Strong prioritization and organizational skills. Possess an analytical mindset and demonstrable critical thinking skills. This job description is not all-inclusive. Macy's, Inc. reserves the right to amend this job description at any time. Macy's, Inc. is an Equal Opportunity Employer, committed to a diverse and inclusive work environment. This position may be eligible for performance-based incentives/bonuses. Benefits include 401k, medical/vision/dental/life/disability insurance options, PTO accruals, Holidays, and more. Eligibility requirements may apply based on location, job level, classification, and length of employment. Click here to see details on benefits.

title: Business Analyst
description: Topix Media Lab is seeking a detail-oriented and highly organized Business Analyst to support our finance, sales, and rights operations. This role plays a critical part in ensuring accurate financial tracking, smooth royalty administration, and reliable sales reporting across the organization. The ideal candidate is proactive, systems-minded, and comfortable juggling multiple work streams while collaborating closely with internal teams and external partners. Key Responsibilities Financial & Accounting Support Review and verify all expenses; maintain and update cash flow and expense trackers. Input invoices and process purchase orders Prepare quarterly journal entries and perform month-end bank reconciliations. Royalty & Rights Administration Input and maintain contract terms in the royalty system. Upload and maintain sales and royalty reports and statements. Coordinate rights system data and ensure ongoing system accuracy. Sales Operations & Reporting Input, distribute, and track sales invoices and receivables. Manage remainder reports. Coordinate and maintain sales account portals. Support sales presentations and reporting needs. Provide trade show coordination and logistical support. Client & Internal Support Provide client support related to sales, rights, and reporting inquiries. Coordinate across teams to ensure accurate and timely sales, rights, and financial information. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Administration, or a related field (or equivalent practical experience). 2+ years of experience in accounting, finance operations, sales operations, or royalty administration. Experience using accounting software; NetSuite experience strongly preferred. Working knowledge of accounting principles, including invoicing, reconciliations, and month-end close processes. Strong proficiency in Excel, including formulas, pivot tables, and data analysis. Experience working with contracts, royalty systems, or rights management platforms preferred. Strong attention to detail with the ability to identify discrepancies and resolve issues. Excellent organizational, communication, and follow-up skills. Ability to support process improvement initiatives, including automation and reporting enhancement This role is fully remote. To apply, send your resume and cover letter to careers@topixmedia.com About Us: Topix Media Lab is an independent publisher that creates newsstand magazines and illustrated nonfiction books covering a range of categories from tabletop roleplaying games and pop culture-themed cookbooks to general reference titles and children's books. We work with iconic brands, including Pokemon, Hasbro, Smithsonian, John Wayne Enterprises, MuggleNet, and others, as well as award-winning and bestselling authors like Sother Teague, Steve Spangler, and more.

title: Senior Business Analyst
description: Quantam Solutions provides IT solutions and consulting for various clients. We offer competitive hourly wages, health benefits, paid time off, and a 401(k) plan. We are currently seeking a Senior Business Analyst . Local candidates within 70 miles of Lansing, MI are strongly preferred. Non-local candidates must be willing to relocate within 70 miles of Lansing, MI at their own expense from acceptance with no delays. Interviews will be in person in Lansing, MI. There is no option for a video interview. The work schedule is hybrid, with two days onsite (Tuesdays and Wednesday) in Lansing, MI. There is no option for a fully remote work schedule. A Bachelor’s Degree is required for this position. Job Description: The primary duty of this Senior Business Analyst position is to act as a liaison among business stakeholders to elicit, analyze, communicate, and validate requirements for changes to information systems, business processes, and policies, as applicable. This position collects, clarifies, and translates business requirements into design documentation, from which applications and solutions are developed. Documentation will include use of standard client application templates and tools, Azure Dev Ops, Visio, and Excel. This position must be able to work with our client’s other business analysts and transfer knowledge to them. The resource must be able to collaborate with business clients, technical teams, Process Modelers, System Analysts, and vendors to clarify design intent, identify gaps, and ensure technical feasibility. Required Skill Sets: Over seven years of experience as a senior business analyst. Experience in a government, retirement system, or financial services environment. Knowledge of Azure DevOps, Ability to write user stories to support Agile development. Knowledge and experience in gathering financial systems requirements. Facilitate, capture, organize and document business requirements from all stakeholders, including business-side employees, product owners, and technical resources. Requirements analysis and the ability to decipher what is said to what is really wanted or needed. Communication and translating requirements between the business and technical teams to ensure shared understanding of requirements and design decisions. Produce understandable yet highly detailed requirements, process flows for the business and technical teams. Create, organize, and maintain wiki content using markup languages such as Markdown, Wiki Markup, or reStructuredText. Develop intuitive wiki structures, including navigation hierarchy, templates, and cross-referenced content, to enhance user accessibility and knowledge continuity. Ensure documentation quality by applying standards, best practices, and version control across wiki content. Support continuous improvement by identifying documentation gaps and proposing enhancements. Lead backlog refinement sessions to ensure user stories meet “Ready” state with clear details and acceptance criteria. Collaborate with project team and business analysts to continuously improve story quality and prioritize work. Participate in Agile ceremonies (Planning, Stand-ups, Reviews, Retrospectives) to support iterative delivery. Familiarity with Agile SDLC, process modeling, and system testing methodologies. Bachelor’s Degree in Business Administration, Information Systems, Public Administration, or a related field. Additional Tasks Related to a Business Analyst Duty Include: Assist with the selection of the strategies, policies, programs, and procedures for achieving the objectives and goals of the development needs. Provide research and input for the development of Statements of Work (SOW) and Total Cost of Ownership (TCO) for selected solutions. Assist with System Security Plans (SSP) when needed. Research and provide input in the creation of SEM documentation. Participate in artifact reviews with peers, system specialists, Enterprise Security and other DTMB entities to ensure IT solutions and applications adhere to DTMB and agency policies, standards, or guidelines. Assist with design and document IT solutions that are State of Michigan standard technology. Work with Project Managers to draft change board documents. Assist in development of training content. Design, develop, and implement test plans for applications or subsystems. Create or collect test data and execute approved tests to determine the accuracy of program logic to produce desired results. Assist in testing and differentiating between defects and new requirements. Maintain/update system documentation for system upgrades. Assist with reviewing UI/UX design artifacts and understanding core design and usability principles. Proficiency in markup languages (e.g., Markdown, Wiki Markup, reStructuredText). Hands-on experience with documentation or collaboration tools, Azure DevOps Wiki.

title: Business Analyst (Starlink Growth)
description: SpaceX was founded under the belief that a future where humanity is out exploring the stars is fundamentally more exciting than one where we are not. Today SpaceX is actively developing the technologies to make this possible, with the ultimate goal of enabling human life on Mars. BUSINESS ANALYST (STARLINK GROWTH) Starlink, our revolutionary satellite constellation, delivers low-latency broadband internet worldwide, connecting consumers in remote and underserved areas. The Starlink Growth team drives consumer adoption and expansion by analyzing market trends, optimizing acquisition strategies, and informing pricing and retention initiatives. This role will focus on consumer metrics and insights, conducting research and reporting to support data-driven decisions that accelerate subscriber growth and enhance user value. Our ideal candidate is a proactive analyst with a blend of quantitative skills and curiosity for market dynamics, capable of turning complex data into actionable strategies for consumer growth. Responsibilities Perform in-depth analyses of consumer metrics, including Lifetime Value (LTV), Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC), churn rates, and cohort performance, using SQL queries and statistical methods to uncover trends and opportunities Develop forecasting models for subscriber growth, revenue projections, and market penetration, incorporating external factors like competitive landscapes and economic indicators Create and maintain reporting dashboards and visualizations (e.g., using Tableau, Power BI, or Looker) to track key performance indicators (KPIs) for consumer acquisition, retention, and engagement Conduct market research and price analysis, evaluating elasticity, competitive pricing, and promotional impacts to recommend optimizations that drive revenue and affordability Collaborate cross-functionally with marketing, product, and sales teams to integrate insights into growth strategies, including A/B testing support and scenario planning Identify data gaps and work with engineering teams to improve data collection for consumer use cases, ensuring robust datasets for ongoing analysis Deliver presentations and reports to stakeholders, translating technical findings into clear, business-oriented recommendations that influence decision-making Support ad-hoc research projects, such as regional market assessments or emerging trends in broadband adoption, blending qualitative insights with quantitative rigor Basic Qualifications Bachelor's degree 2+ years of professional experience in business analytics, consulting, market research, or growth operations 1+ years of experience with SQL and creating/managing dashboards using data visualization tools (e.g. Tableau, Power BI, Looker) Preferred Skills And Experience Bachelor's degree in business, economics, statistics, computer science, or a related quantitative field Advanced SQL skills, including complex joins, window functions, and query optimization Experience with forecasting, financial metrics (e.g., LTV, CAC), or pricing analysis Proficiency in statistical modeling and forecasting techniques (e.g., time-series analysis, regression in Python or R) Experience with ETL processes, data pipelines, or automation scripting (e.g., Python, VBA) Background in high-growth consumer tech environments, startups, or consulting firms with a focus on customer acquisition and retention Strong research skills, including familiarity with market intelligence tools (e.g., surveys, competitive analysis platforms) Excellent communication abilities, with a track record of presenting insights to non-technical audiences and influencing cross-functional teams Project management experience, including prioritizing tasks in fast-paced settings and collaborating with diverse stakeholders Additional Requirements Position may involve extended hours and weekend work as needed This position may require travel between various SpaceX sites Pay Range COMPENSATION AND BENEFITS: Business Analyst/Level I: $75,000.00 - $95,000.00/per year Business Analyst/Level II: $90,000.00 - $115,000.00/per year Your actual level and base salary will be determined on a case-by-case basis and may vary based on the following considerations: job-related knowledge and skills, education, and experience. title: Business Operations Analyst
description: About Baselayer Trusted by 2,200+ financial institutions, Baselayer is the intelligent business identity platform that helps verify any business, automate KYB, and monitor real-time risk. Baselayer’s B2B risk solutions and identity graph network leverage state and federal government filings and proprietary data sources to prevent fraud, accelerate onboarding, and lower credit losses. We are a fast-growing SaaS company operating in a highly regulated space, focused on building durable infrastructure, best-in-class processes, and a culture of ownership and execution. About the Role We are looking for a Senior Finance & Business Intelligence Analyst to play a foundational role in building and scaling Baselayer’s financial and operational intelligence. This role will work across all functions including Finance, Operations, Sales, Product, and Engineering to improve how the company measures performance, makes decisions, and scales. This is a highly visible role reporting directly to the Head of Operations & Finance. You will own core financial processes, business intelligence tooling, and operational reporting, while proactively identifying opportunities to improve systems, workflows, and data integrity. You will also help develop recurring executive and board-level reporting. This is not a plug-and-play analyst role. We are looking for someone who wants to build, improve, and own best-in-class financial and operational processes from the ground up. What You’ll Do Financial and Operational Processes Build, maintain, and continuously improve core financial and operational processes, including reporting, forecasting inputs, and performance tracking Develop, maintain, and improve monthly, quarterly, and board-level financial and operational reports Partner with Finance and Operations leadership to support budgeting, forecasting, KPI development, and executive reporting Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and auditability across financial and operational systems Business Intelligence and Analytics Own and enhance Metabase dashboards, ensuring they are accurate, actionable, and trusted across the company Design and maintain SQL-based data models and reporting views to support company-wide decision-making Translate ambiguous business questions into clear analytical frameworks and insights Systems and Tooling Drive improvements in HubSpot reporting, tracking, and data structure in partnership with Sales, Marketing, and RevOps Identify gaps and inefficiencies across tools and workflows, and proactively propose solutions Act as a connective layer between Finance, GTM, Product, and Engineering when data or systems need improvement Cross-Functional Collaboration Work closely with leaders and individual contributors across the company to understand metrics, surface insights, and improve operational clarity Help establish consistent definitions, metrics, and reporting standards across teams Serve as a trusted analytical partner who brings structure and clarity to complex problems About You You are highly analytical, deeply curious, and motivated by building systems that scale. You thrive in environments with ownership, ambiguity, and high expectations. Required Experience and Skills 2 to 5 years of experience in Finance, Business Intelligence, FP&A, Analytics, or a related role, ideally in a SaaS or technology-driven company Highly advanced Microsoft Excel skills Highly advanced SQL skills, with experience writing complex, performant queries Hands-on experience building and maintaining dashboards, Metabase strongly preferred Experience working with CRM or GTM systems, HubSpot strongly preferred Experience preparing executive-level and board-facing reporting is strongly preferred What Sets You Apart You think out of the box and enjoy improving how things work rather than maintaining the status quo You are proactive, self-directed, and comfortable operating without detailed instructions You care deeply about data quality, consistency, and trust You communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders You want to be foundational and help build something that lasts Work Location Remote or hybrid in our NYC office Compensation and Benefits Base salary range of $70,000 to $125,000 Equity package Unlimited vacation Comprehensive health, dental, and vision coverage 401(k) with company match

title: AI Business Analyst
description: Business Analyst – Automation & AI Howden Group is committed to attracting and developing exceptional talent. We believe that our people are our greatest competitive advantage, and we strive to create an environment where everyone can grow, contribute, and build a long‑term career. Internal mobility is a key part of this philosophy, and we encourage colleagues to explore new opportunities within the Group. We are now seeking a Business Analyst to play a pivotal role in an exciting AI‑driven transformation initiative within our new US Retail insurance broking business. This is a unique opportunity to shape the future of our US operations by embedding automation, AI insights, and next‑generation productivity tools across the organisation. Role Summary The Business Analyst will act as the bridge between business teams, data specialists, technology partners, and solution providers. You will lead analysis across multiple functional domains, ensuring that AI and automation capabilities are designed, prioritised, and delivered in a way that enhances efficiency, improves customer experience, and supports our strategic growth ambitions. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in a fast‑paced, entrepreneurial environment and is passionate about using AI to solve real business problems. The Business Analyst will also play a key role in identifying and articulating the art of the possible with Generative AI (GenAI) and intelligent automation, translating emerging AI capabilities into practical, business-understandable opportunities that align with Howden’s strategic objectives and operating model evolution. Key Responsibilities Lead workshops, interviews, and prototyping sessions to elicit, validate, and refine business requirements. Conduct detailed analysis across functional areas within the US retail broking business. Collaborate closely with technology and solution providers to shape and evaluate AI‑enabled solutions. Produce high‑quality analysis artefacts including user stories, functional specifications, acceptance criteria, wireframes, process flows, and capability models. Drive prioritisation of requirements in partnership with business stakeholders and product owners. Provide clear, structured communication to project teams and senior leadership. Champion a delivery‑focused, proactive approach to analysis within a startup‑style environment. Translate complex AI concepts into clear, business-friendly language, helping stakeholders understand practical applications, risks, limitations, and expected value. Conduct AI opportunity assessments, including feasibility analysis, value estimation, data readiness evaluation, and risk considerations. Define future-state, AI-enabled business processes incorporating automation, copilots, knowledge assistants, document intelligence, and agentic task orchestration. Partner with Data, Architecture, and Information Security teams to ensure AI solutions align with governance, compliance, responsible AI standards, and data protection requirements. Support experimentation and prototyping of GenAI solutions, including prompt design, workflow modelling, and human-in-the-loop validation approaches. Help establish structured frameworks for evaluating AI initiatives, including measurable success criteria, adoption metrics, and value realization tracking. Required Experience & Skills Proven experience as a Lead Business Analyst, Consultant, Product Owner, or similar role within a US insurance broker, carrier, or consultancy. Strong understanding of end‑to‑end US retail broking processes, including placement, invoicing, certificate issuance, accounting, and settlement. Experience working on large‑scale business transformation programmes within the insurance sector. Demonstrated ability to produce high‑quality analysis deliverables and translate complex requirements into actionable solutions. Excellent communication and presentation skills, with experience engaging senior stakeholders. Highly self‑directed, delivery‑oriented, and comfortable operating with minimal supervision. Ability to thrive in a dynamic, fast‑moving environment and contribute to a collaborative team culture. Demonstrated experience identifying and translating AI opportunities into tangible business process improvements. ServiceNow experience Ability to articulate the “art of the possible” with AI in clear, non-technical language to business stakeholders. Experience working with AI-driven tools such as Microsoft Copilot, ChatGPT-style assistants, document intelligence solutions, or workflow automation platforms is highly desirable. Awareness of AI governance, ethical considerations, data privacy implications, and model risk management within regulated industries. Location Remote, with occasional domestic travel as required. Our Culture: People First Since opening our first office in 1994, Howden has grown from a London‑based specialist broker to the world’s largest independent insurance brokerage, with more than 15,000 colleagues across 100+ territories. Despite this scale, our values remain unchanged: • Employee ownership • Aligned external investors • Trust, integrity, and long‑term relationships • Deep expertise • Independence Our people‑first culture is built on collaboration, innovation, and empowerment. Employee ownership gives our colleagues a real stake in the business and fosters an entrepreneurial spirit that sets us apart. When we bring in great people and help them thrive, everyone benefits. Diversity & Inclusion We believe diversity makes us stronger. We treat all colleagues, candidates, clients, and partners with fairness, respect, and equality—regardless of age, disability, race, religion or belief, gender, sexual orientation, marital status, or family circumstances.

Elligint Health integrates vast amounts of data from across the healthcare continuum, delivering intelligence that informs decision-making, enhances care coordination, and improves outcomes. Focused on enabling actionable intervention and whole-person care, Elligint Health helps healthcare organizations navigate complexity, turning insights into strategies that benefit providers, payers, and members and patients alike. With Elligint Health, the future of healthcare is simpler, smarter, and more effective. Position Summary We are seeking an experienced Business Analyst with Payor Clinical Workflow expertise to join our team supporting the Helios® platform implementation and optimization. This role will focus on analyzing, designing, and improving business processes and workflows to ensure effective utilization of Helios® for our clients and internal stakeholders. Duties & Responsibilities Collaborate with business users, implementation teams, and technical resources to gather, document, and analyze business requirements related to workflows in Helios®. Assess current business processes and identify opportunities for workflow automation, optimization, and standardization. Design and document workflow solutions, business process maps, user stories and functional requirements aligned with Helios® capabilities. Work closely with the configuration and development teams to ensure requirements are clearly understood and delivered as designed. Develop and maintain process flows, functional specifications, and gap analyses to support Helios® enhancements and implementations. Facilitate requirements workshops, process design sessions, and workflow validation meetings with business and technical teams. Participate in system testing, user acceptance testing (UAT), and defect triaging, ensuring workflows meet business needs. Support training materials and change management efforts related to new or modified workflows. Serve as a subject matter expert on workflow functionality within Helios®, advising stakeholders on best practices and solution approaches. Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree. 3+ years of business analysis experience, preferably in healthcare technology or SaaS implementations. Strong experience with Payor Clinical workflow design, process mapping, and process reengineering. Proven ability to gather requirements, write clear user stories and functional specifications, and facilitate effective meetings. Experience with workflow diagramming tools such as Visio, Lucidchart, or similar. Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills. Team-oriented and collaborative working style. Ability to work effectively with cross-functional teams in a fast-paced environment to support assigned programs. Must reside in the U.S.; we are unable to provide visa sponsorship at this time. Preferred Qualifications Experience with Helios® (or comparative care management or utilization management platforms) implementations or workflow optimization projects. Knowledge of healthcare operations, care management workflows, and regulatory requirements. Familiarity with Agile methodologies, JIRA, Confluence, and related tools. Compensation Competitive salary & equity in Elligint Health PTO and company sick leave Health, dental, and vision insurance 401(k) participation and matching Opportunity to join a rapidly growing company and shape its future Elligint Health is committed to ensuring that information security remains a top priority for everyone. All workers are responsible for the protection of our Information Security, and we take the execution of this seriously. Information Security Policies and procedures details and training will be provided during onboarding. Each candidate will be subject to a drug screening, background check, and reference check before beginning employment. Please note that some of our positions require U.S. citizenship and submission and further approval of “Public Trust” federal clearance.

title: Business Analyst
description: Responsibilities Kforce has a client in Coral Gables, FL that is seeking a Business Analyst. Responsibilities: Document the systems requirements for both projects and production support in a matter that is clear to both the Process Owners and IT functional and technical resources Work in conjunction with the Process Owners and Subject Matter Experts to ensure the designed solution meets the need and provides the expected quality and helps achieve the desired benefits Negotiate alternatives when presented with systems limitations and to avoid scope creep Write test plans and lead the testing effort, and execute test cases Assist IT resources with the troubleshooting of issues and possible missing requirements Support the implementation of the solution and monitor its execution in the Production environment before full hand-off to the Process Owners On-going support and troubleshooting of unexpected system issues Mentoring of junior analysts on the team Requirements To be considered for this position, candidates must have experience in a similar role, or they must possess significant knowledge, experience, and abilities to successfully perform the responsibilities listed Relevant education and/or training will be considered a plus The pay range is the lowest to highest compensation we reasonably in good faith believe we would pay at posting for this role. We may ultimately pay more or less than this range. Employee pay is based on factors like relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, location, performance, union contract and business needs. This range may be modified in the future. We offer comprehensive benefits including medical/dental/vision insurance, HSA, FSA, 401(k), and life, disability & ADD insurance to eligible employees. Salaried personnel receive paid time off. Hourly employees are not eligible for paid time off unless required by law. Hourly employees on a Service Contract Act project are eligible for paid sick leave. Note: Pay is not considered compensation until it is earned, vested and determinable. The amount and availability of any compensation remains in Kforce's sole discretion unless and until paid and may be modified in its discretion consistent with the law. This job is not eligible for bonuses, incentives or commissions. Kforce is an Equal Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, protected veteran status, or disability status. By clicking “Apply Today” you agree to receive calls, AI-generated calls, text messages or emails from Kforce and its affiliates, and service providers. Note that if you choose to communicate with Kforce via text messaging the frequency may vary, and message and data rates may apply. Carriers are not liable for delayed or undelivered messages. title: Business Control Analyst
description: THE POSITION Our roster has an opening with your name on it Seeking a highly motivated individual to join the Operational Excellence team’s business control function. This role will contribute to the identification, assessment, and mitigation of business risks across FanDuel's operational teams. The analyst will work closely with business stakeholders, enterprise risk function, and internal audit to ensure effective control design and implementation with a focus on business resiliency. In addition to the specific responsibilities outlined above, employees may be required to perform other such duties as assigned by the Company. This ensures operational flexibility and allows the Company to meet evolving business needs. THE GAME PLAN Everyone on our team has a part to play General Overview: Take ownership of documenting business controls, conducting process walkthroughs, and analyzing control effectiveness Proactively identify risks and issues, implement mitigating strategies and controls to reduce future likelihood and impact Assist the team in evaluating business processes, anticipating requirements, uncovering areas for improvement, as well as developing and implementing solutions Design controls that effectively mitigate identified risks through process walkthroughs, building process maps and stakeholder collaboration Develop project plans and timelines related to risk mitigation initiatives Deliver high quality insights using quantitative and qualitative analysis to make recommendations that balance customer and business impact Advocate for change by establishing strong stakeholder relationships Document and communicate key insights or recommendations to wide audiences, from peers to senior leadership THE STATS What we're looking for in our next teammate Required Qualifications: Expected completion of degree or program by June 2025 Must have U.S. Work Authorization and does not require employer sponsorship Must be in commutable distance from or willing to relocate to designated office Well-organized and meticulous attention to detail Solution-oriented, adaptable and efficient learner Collaborative team player who is comfortable working with a diverse array of partners and stakeholders across multiple functions Proficiency in process mapping, detailed documentation, project planning, and data analysis Preferred Qualifications: Interest in eCommerce and/or iGaming industries preferred Understanding of risk management frameworks Familiarity with Confluence, Jira, Miro, LucidChart, AuditBoard Risk assessment experience a plus Don’t check all the boxes? That’s okay! We encourage you to still apply if you feel like you possess an adjacent skillet and are interested in learning more about this position. About Fanduel FanDuel Group is the premier mobile gaming company in the United States and Canada. FanDuel Group consists of a portfolio of leading brands across mobile wagering including: America’s #1 Sportsbook, FanDuel Sportsbook; its leading iGaming platform, FanDuel Casino; the industry’s unquestioned leader in horse racing and advance-deposit wagering, FanDuel Racing; and its daily fantasy sports product. In addition, FanDuel Group operates FanDuel TV, its broadly distributed linear cable television network and FanDuel TV+, its leading direct-to-consumer OTT platform. FanDuel Group has a presence across all 50 states, Canada, and Puerto Rico. The company is based in New York with US offices in Los Angeles, Atlanta, and Jersey City, as well as global offices in Canada and Scotland. The company’s affiliates have offices worldwide, including in Ireland, Portugal, Romania, and Australia. FanDuel Group is a subsidiary of Flutter Entertainment, the world's largest sports betting and gaming operator with a portfolio of globally recognized brands and traded on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: FLUT). Player Benefits We treat our team right We offer amazing benefits above and beyond the basics. We have an array of health plans to choose from (some as low as $0 per paycheck) that include programs for fertility and family planning, mental health support, and fitness benefits. We offer generous paid time off (PTO & sick leave), annual bonus and long-term incentive opportunities (based on performance), 401k with up to a 5% match, commuter benefits , pet insurance, and more - check out all our benefits here: FanDuel Total Rewards . *Benefits differ across location, role, and level. FanDuel is an equal opportunities employer and we believe, as one of our principles states, “We are One Team!”. As such, we are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ethnicity, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship status, marital status, disability, gender identity, gender expression, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by state, local or federal law. We believe FanDuel is strongest and best able to compete if all employees feel valued, respected, and included. The applicable salary range for this position is $79,000 - $99,000 USD, which is dependent on a variety of factors including relevant experience, location, business needs and market demand. This role may offer the following benefits: medical, vision, and dental insurance; life insurance; disability insurance; a 401(k) matching program; among other employee benefits. This role may also be eligible for short-term or long-term incentive compensation, including, but not limited to, cash bonuses and stock program participation. This role includes paid personal time off and 14 paid company holidays. FanDuel offers paid sick time in accordance with all applicable state and federal laws. FanDuel is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please email Benefits@fanduel.com. It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.

title: Business Analyst
description: About Calpion is an 18-year-old Dallas-headquartered technology firm that offers customers artificial intelligence solutions by building custom deep learning and machine learning algorithms, custom enterprise application development and maintenance, RPA solutions, cloud services, Salesforce consulting, customized invoice management software solutions, and other technology services. We also offer automated bot-driven medical billing services. Our healthcare offering includes a Salesforce platform-based practice management and billing software with automated patient e-statements and a payment portal. Along With Being SOC-certified And HIPPA-compliant, We Have Certified experts in deep learning & machine learning to provide intelligent solutions for your business. Helped Fortune 500 companies build custom enterprise-level applications. Provide remote & accurate AutoCAD Drawing, Drafting, 2D &3D modeling, and design services. Strategic partnerships with Microsoft, Amazon, SAP, HP, Worksoft, Microfocus, Tricentis, etc. Partnered with UiPath to provide RPA solutions. We are headquartered in Dallas with multiple global Centers of Excellence around the industry. Focus: Healthcare, Logistics, Supply chain, Biotechnology, Hospitality, Manufacturing, and Airlines. ‍ Life at Calpion Inc. Calpion offers a unique work environment that is both thrilling and enriching, fostering personal and professional growth. Our company is a hub of innovation, collaboration, and continuous learning, where we encourage our employees to adopt a positive mindset and strive for excellence. At Calpion, you'll be part of a vibrant team that thrives on creativity and problem-solving. You'll have the chance to work on cutting-edge projects, harnessing the latest technologies and methodologies to deliver intelligent solutions that make a real difference for our clients. Calpion prioritizes the well-being of its employees and fosters a supportive and inclusive culture that promotes work-life balance. If you are enthusiastic about joining a vibrant organization that values your input, Calpion is the ideal place to pursue your career goals. ‍ Job Title: Business analyst Report To: Project Manager Experience: 5+ Years Qualification: Any Degree Location: Dallas Shift Time: Regular(8-5 PM) Mode: WFO Terms-Fulltime/Part time/Contractual: Full-Time ‍ Job Summary As a Business Analyst, your responsibilities include assessing organizations' performance and helping them improve their processes and systems. Business analysts conduct research and analysis to devise solutions to business problems and help introduce these solutions to businesses and their clients. ‍ Key Responsibilities Prior experience working in Medical Billing, Practice Management, EMR, and Patient Portal products/applications is required. Responsible for gathering requirements, performing technical analysis, and developing product/project documentation. Work closely with developers during the development phase to clarify requirements. Work with product/project managers and external customers to analyze business opportunities and develop solutions that enable users to satisfy business objectives. ‍ Mandatory Skills Work on technical analysis and troubleshooting for existing products and support client requests for defects and enhancements. Work with QA to create test plans and regression test suites. Identify changes to processes or systems to increase the client's value. Give demos to stakeholders on newly developed features and maintain backlog. Excellent written, verbal, and presentation skills are a must. ‍ Talk about our culture and values At Calpion, we're not just a company—we're a dynamic culture fueled by six core values: Agile, Collaborative, Innovative, Fun, Inclusive, and Passionate. These values drive our every move: Agile: We thrive on change, adapting swiftly to new challenges. Collaborative: Together, we achieve greatness through teamwork and diverse perspectives. Innovative: We push boundaries, constantly exploring new ideas and solutions. Fun: Laughter and camaraderie make our workplace a joyous one. Inclusive: Diversity is our strength, ensuring every voice is heard and valued. Passionate: We approach every task with dedication and enthusiasm. Join us at Calpion and be part of a culture that's not just about work—it's about innovation, growth, and making an impact.

title: Business Analyst
description: Business Analyst (Remote – United States) Position Summary We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Business Analyst to support data-driven decision-making and operational improvements across the organization. This remote U.S.-based position will serve as a key liaison between business stakeholders and technical teams, ensuring business requirements are clearly defined, analyzed, and translated into effective solutions. The ideal candidate possesses strong analytical skills, business acumen, and the ability to communicate insights effectively to diverse audiences. Key Responsibilities Gather, analyze, and document business requirements from stakeholders Translate business needs into functional specifications and process documentation Conduct data analysis to identify trends, risks, and opportunities Develop reports, dashboards, and performance metrics to support strategic decisions Collaborate with cross-functional teams including IT, operations, finance, and leadership Evaluate existing business processes and recommend improvements Support project management activities including scope definition, testing, and implementation Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and compliance with company standards Assist in change management and user training initiatives as needed Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, Information Systems, or related field 2–4 years of experience in business analysis, data analysis, or related roles Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and data analysis tools Experience with reporting platforms such as Power BI, Tableau, or similar tools Familiarity with ERP or CRM systems is preferred Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and critical-thinking skills Strong written and verbal communication abilities Ability to manage multiple priorities and work independently in a remote environment

title: Senior Business Analyst
description: Job Title: Senior Business Analyst Location: Remote (work from home eligible) Salary: $100,000 - $120,000 annually DOE Company Overview: Celerity is a consulting firm specializing in the utilities and energy sectors. We empower organizations by converting complex data into actionable intelligence to mitigate risk, optimize assets, and maximize project outcomes. Our background and expertise enable us to drive and deliver transformation and operational excellence for our clients. Our background and expertise enable us to deliver Field Service Management (FSM) solutions, driving digital transformation and operational excellence for our clients. Position Overview: We are seeking a Senior Business Analyst with over 5 years of experience in Field Service Management tools and a strong background in the utilities and energy industries. The ideal candidate will have a consulting background and will play a pivotal role in analyzing client business processes, identifying requirements, and implementing FSM solutions to enhance operational efficiency. Key Responsibilities: Requirements Gathering and Analysis: Engage with clients to understand and document their business processes, needs, and objectives related to field service operations Analyze and translate business requirements into functional specifications for FSM solutions Conduct gap analyses to identify areas where current systems fall short of client needs Solution Design and Implementation: Collaborate with technical teams to design FSM solutions that align with client requirements and industry best practices Assist in configuring and customizing FSM tools, particularly OverIT's platform, to meet specific client needs Develop and execute test plans to ensure solutions meet specified requirements and quality standards Stakeholder Communication and Management: Serve as a liaison between clients, project managers, and technical teams to ensure clear communication and alignment throughout the project lifecycle Facilitate workshops and meetings to gather requirements, present solutions, and obtain stakeholder buy-in Prepare and deliver presentations and reports to clients and internal stakeholders Process Improvement and Optimization: Identify opportunities to enhance client business processes through the effective use of FSM solutions Recommend best practices and innovative solutions to improve field service operations and efficiency Monitor solution performance and suggest enhancements based on data analysis and client feedback Training and Support: Develop training materials and conduct training sessions for clients to ensure successful adoption of FSM solutions Provide ongoing support to clients post-implementation to address issues and optimize solution usage Educational Qualifications: Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field Advanced degree (MBA or Master’s) is preferred Required Skills and Experience: Minimum of 5 years of experience as a Business Analyst, with a focus on Field Service Management solutions in the utilities and energy industries Demonstrated experience in requirements gathering, process analysis, and solution implementation Familiarity with business integration requirements across technology stack (ERP, CRM, CIS, BI, Datawarehouse, etc.) Hands-on experience with SQL to access, retrieve, manipulate, and analyze data Strong understanding of utility operations, asset management, and regulatory compliance Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to think critically and propose effective solutions Proficiency in using FSM software platforms; experience with OverIT's solutions is a plus Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage effectively with clients and stakeholders at all levels Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines Ability to travel to client site (domestic and(or) international) as required and defined per project requirements Preferred Skills: Consulting background with experience in client-facing roles and managing stakeholder relationships Familiarity with GIS integration and mobile workforce management solutions Knowledge of emerging technologies such as IoT, AI, and Machine Learning as they apply to Field Service Management Professional certifications related to Business Analysis (e.g., CBAP) or Project Management (e.g., PMP) Salary: $100,000 - $120,000 annually DOE The salary range for this position is provided in good faith and is subject to variation based on geographic location, candidate experience, and market conditions. Final compensation will be determined after a comprehensive evaluation of the candidate’s qualifications and alignment with the role. In accordance with applicable pay transparency laws—including but not limited to those in California, Colorado, New York, Washington, and other jurisdictions—we disclose salary ranges to promote equity and transparency. Where required by law, compensation and benefits information will be included in job postings or made available upon request. Employees may be asked to perform additional duties that are reasonably related to their role and consistent with company policies and applicable labor laws. title: Business Analyst
description: NXT is looking for a Business Analyst with expertise in systems development to join our team. The Business Analyst will work with a talented and diverse team to support complex IT project initiatives. As a Business Analyst, you will play a pivotal role in driving strategic initiatives, optimizing operational efficiency, and delivering innovative solutions for clients. Responsibilities Collaborate closely with agency stakeholders to understand business objectives, challenges, and requirements Conduct comprehensive analysis to gather, document, and validate business requirements, ensuring alignment with project objectives and industry standards Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather business requirements for system enhancements and drive the implementation of solutions Utilize quantitative and qualitative analysis techniques to interpret complex data sets, identify patterns, and derive actionable insights Conduct comprehensive analysis to understand the current system's architecture, processes, and limitations Identify areas for improvement and efficiency gains, and work with stakeholders to gather requirements for the project Develop comprehensive business requirements, use cases, and process flows for new systems Act as a liaison between business stakeholders and IT teams to ensure successful system transformation results Conduct risk assessments and impact analyses to evaluate potential risks and opportunities associated with proposed solutions or business changes Contribute to project planning, monitoring, and execution activities, ensuring timely delivery of high-quality solutions within budget and scope Develop and execute change management strategies and plans to facilitate a smooth transition to new systems Create detailed documentation of business processes, system requirements, and change management plans Analyze data and generate meaningful insights to inform decision-making processes Provide data-driven recommendations and insights to improve performance and efficiency Maintain documentation to ensure alignment with evolving business needs and regulatory requirements Collaborate with IT teams, business teams, and other stakeholders to bridge the gap between business needs and system capabilities Must Haves – Required Skills Bachelor's degree in computer science, information systems, or related field 5+ years’ experience as a Business Analyst Strong analytical skills with proficiency in data analysis tools and techniques (e.g., SQL, Excel, Tableau) Ability to translate business requirements into actionable process improvements Experience working in an Agile Environment Experience with process improvement methodologies such as Lean Six Sigma or ITIL Continual Service Improvement Excellent communication skills with the ability to effectively communicate complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders Consulting experience Nice To Haves – Desired Skills Relevant industry certifications (e.g., CBAP, PMP, Lean Six Sigma) Location: Hybrid: Client site and Remote Benefits: Why you want to work with us! We are a young and vibrant organization with a strong foundation and looking for the next set of leaders to grow the business. Fully company-paid healthcare for you and your family Monthly expense of $100 paid towards health and wellness expenses of your choice – gym, yoga classes, massages, etc. Monthly reimbursement for phone bill up to $100 Unlimited paid annual leave Work from anywhere as long as quality work is getting done Flexible hours – take breaks, cater to your personal and family appointments during the day as needed, core hours 9-4 We want our employees to be eternal learners – we will pay for reasonable and relevant trainings and certifications We want you to participate in the company’s growth – healthy bonus and performance-based commissions to be discussed during the hiring process depending on the applicability to the role NXT is an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity. All employment is decided on the basis of qualifications, merit and business need. JOB CODE: 1000027

title: Business Analyst
description: Business Analyst Analytics, Strategy & Decision Support Location : NEW JERSEY / NOT REMOTE MUST BE LOCATED IN NJ Role Summary We are looking for a high-performing Business Analyst with strong analytical skills, excellent Excel and slide-building ability, and experience creating dashboards and business intelligence tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar). This role is ideal for someone with consulting-style problem solving, comfort working with unstructured datasets, and the ability to support cross-functional leaders with clear, structured insights. You will work directly with the VP of Analytics & Technology to develop metrics and dashboards, build models, support operational and financial analysis, and contribute to strategic decisions across a growing healthcare organization. Key Responsibilities Dashboarding & Business Intelligence Build, refine, and maintain dashboards in Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar BI platforms. Use strong data modeling fundamentals to create reliable, scalable reporting. Build KPI dashboards for operations, staffing, finance, HR, and clinical programs. Collaborate with the VP of Analytics & Technology on data source integration and automation. Analytical & Financial Modeling Build advanced Excel models, including scenario analyses, forecasting tools, staffing models, and revenue/cost models. Perform structured data analysis to support decisions around operations, staffing, clinical productivity, compensation, and growth. Validate, clean, and transform raw data from EMR, billing, HRIS, payroll, CRM, and other systems. Executive Reporting & Communication Create executive-ready PowerPoint materials for leadership meetings, board updates, and strategy sessions. Synthesize complex operational or financial data into clear narratives with visual clarity. Organize findings into structured frameworks. Project & Cross-Functional Support Support strategic initiatives across Operations, HR, Finance, Clinical Services, and Business Development. Participate in workflow mapping, requirements gathering, and process documentation for new tools or operational improvements. Assist in building performance metrics, process dashboards, and visibility tools. Required Qualifications Technical Strong proficiency in Excel (Power Query a plus) and PowerPoint. Skilled with modern BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, Qlik, etc.). Experience with automation tools to improve workflow efficiency (Power App, Power Automate, etc.) Ability to structure, clean, and analyze large datasets. Experience 2-5 years in: Management consulting FP&A or corporate strategy Healthcare analytics Business intelligence or data analysis roles Proven ability to work cross-functionally with senior leaders. Experience delivering insights in fast-paced, ambiguous environments. Mindset Strong attention to detail and commitment to accuracy. Analytical, curious, resourceful, and comfortable with incomplete information. High ownership attitude with excellent follow-through. Preferred (Strong Plus, but Not Required) SQL proficiency for querying and analyzing data Exposure to healthcare operations: census, utilization, staffing models, payer mix, EMR data, etc. Experience with workflow mapping or process improvement. Familiarity with Python/R for light analysis or automation (not required).

description: About Clarus Group: Clarus Group is a leading provider of IT services and solutions, delivering cutting-edge technology to drive business success. This role requires the analyst to be on client site daily with no options for remote work. description: It's fun to work in a company where people truly BELIEVE in what they are doing! We're committed to bringing passion and customer focus to the business. Fractal is a strategic AI partner to Fortune 500 companies with a vision to power every human decision in the enterprise. Fractal is building a world where individual choices, freedom, and diversity are the greatest assets; an ecosystem where human imagination is at the heart of every decision. Where no possibility is written off, only challenged to get better. We believe that a true Fractalite is the one who empowers imagination with intelligence. Fractal has been featured as a Great Place to Work by The Economic Times in partnership with the Great Place to Work® Institute and recognized as a ‘Cool Vendor’ and a ‘Vendor to Watch’ by Gartner. Please visit Fractal | Intelligence for Imagination for more information about Fractal. Role Summary Act as the onsite bridge between client business teams, carrier partners, and the data & analytics organization. Leverage first principle thinking, data to generate quick, actionable insights, align technical solutions with business needs, and drive measurable improvements in carrier performance and cost efficiency. Key Responsibilities Partner with senior stakeholders to drive various initiative for D&A org Translate business needs into technical requirements for analytics and engineering teams. Analyse data to deliver rapid, insight-driven recommendations leveraging First Principle Approach Coordinate with carriers on performance reviews, improvement plans, and contract compliance. Identify automation and AI opportunities across business domains Serve as primary onsite liaison, ensuring smooth collaboration between business and technical teams. Qualifications 5–10 years’ experience in business analysis, manufacturing preferred. Strong Problem Solving skills Strong stakeholder management and client-facing experience. Proficient in SQL, Excel, and BI tools (Power BI/Tableau). Excellent communication and problem-solving skills Pay The wage range for this role takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At Fractal, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is: $100k - $131k. In addition, you may be eligible for a discretionary bonus for the current performance period. Benefits As a full-time employee of the company or as an hourly employee working more than 30 hours per week, you will be eligible to participate in the health, dental, vision, life insurance, and disability plans in accordance with the plan documents, which may be amended from time to time. You will be eligible for benefits on the first day of employment with the Company. In addition, you are eligible to participate in the Company 401(k) Plan after 30 days of employment, in accordance with the applicable plan terms. The Company provides for 11 paid holidays and 12 weeks of Parental Leave. We also follow a “free time” PTO policy, allowing you the flexibility to take the time needed for either sick time or vacation. Fractal provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment and prohibits discrimination and harassment of any type without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, national origin, disability status, genetics, protected veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local laws.
