title: Senior Business Intelligence Developer
description: eNGINE builds Technical Teams. We are a Solutions and Placement firm shaped by decades of interaction with Technical professionals. Our inspiration is continuous learning and engagement with the markets we serve, the talent we represent, and the teams we build. Our Consulting Workforce is encouraged to enjoy career fulfillment in the form of challenging projects, schedule flexibility, and paid training/certifications. Successful outcomes start and finish with eNGINE . eNGINE is hiring a highly skilled Senior Power BI Developer to design, develop, and optimize enterprise-level business intelligence solutions. This role requires deep expertise across the full BI stack — including the data layer, semantic layer, and visualization layer — to ensure metric consistency, performance, and data integrity across all reporting platforms. The ideal candidate brings advanced Power BI development experience, strong SQL expertise, and a strong understanding of scalable data modeling and reporting architecture. Key Responsibilities: Design, develop, and maintain advanced Power BI dashboards and reports for enterprise stakeholders Develop complex DAX measures, calculated columns, and optimized data models Architect and manage the semantic layer to ensure consistent KPIs and metric definitions across the organization Partner with data engineering teams to design and optimize the data layer for reporting and analytics use cases Write and optimize complex SQL queries, views, and stored procedures Improve report performance through model optimization, query tuning, and best-practice implementation Ensure data integrity, validation, and reconciliation across reporting solutions Translate business requirements into scalable, maintainable BI solutions Establish and document BI standards, governance, and best practices Required Qualifications DAX (advanced calculations, performance tuning) Data modeling (star schema, snowflake schema, best practices) Report optimization and performance tuning Data layer architecture (warehousing concepts, ETL/ELT processes) Semantic layer design and governance Visualization best practices and storytelling with data Strong SQL experience (query optimization, complex joins, aggregations, window functions) Experience ensuring KPI alignment and metric consistency across enterprise reporting Ability to troubleshoot performance issues across the BI stack Preferred Qualifications Experience working in enterprise or highly regulated environments Familiarity with Azure, Fabric, or modern cloud data platforms Experience implementing BI governance frameworks Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders No C2C, relocation, or sponsorship for these roles. For finer details on how eNGINE can impact your career, apply today!

title: Business Intelligence Developer
description: Summary: STX Beef Co. is seeking a talented and detail-oriented Business Intelligence (BI) Developer to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining BI solutions that empower data-driven decision-making across our organization. This role requires expertise in Power BI, SQL, Excel, and Azure, as well as strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Key Responsibilities: · Design, build, and deploy interactive Power BI dashboards and reports to visualize business metrics and KPIs. · Develop, optimize, and maintain SQL queries, stored procedures, and database objects to support data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) processes. · Integrate and manage data from multiple sources. · Collaborate with business stakeholders to understand requirements, translate them into technical specifications, and deliver actionable insights. · Ensure data quality, consistency, and integrity across all BI solutions. · Monitor and troubleshoot BI systems, addressing performance issues and implementing enhancements as needed. · Document BI processes, data models, and workflows for knowledge sharing and compliance purposes. Required Qualifications: · Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Systems, or related field, or equivalent work experience. · Proven experience developing BI solutions with Power BI, including DAX, Power Query, Dataflows, and Semantic Models. · Strong proficiency in SQL for querying, data modeling, and database management. · Advanced skills in Microsoft Excel, including formulas, VBA, and macros. · Hands-on experience with Azure data services. · Excellent analytical, troubleshooting, and communication skills. · Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. If you are passionate about transforming data into actionable insights and have a strong background in Power BI, SQL, Excel, and Azure, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity! EOE/M/F/Vet/Disabled

title: Business Intelligence (BI) Developer
description: About IEM Industrial Electric Mfg. (IEM) is the largest independent full-line manufacturer of custom power distribution systems in North America. Founded more than 75 years ago in Fremont, California, IEM grew alongside Silicon Valley and today operates over 1.7 million square feet of manufacturing capacity. With $1B+ in annual sales and 10,000+ commissioned projects across technology, data centers, commercial, energy, utilities, healthcare, industrial, and infrastructure markets, IEM continues to deliver exceptional product quality, dependable service, and the flexibility to meet complex technical requirements at scale. Business Intelligence Developer Location: Remote US Reports To: Manager of Business Intelligence Salary Range: $120,000 - $140,000 Annually Position Summary Collaborate with our newly formed Business Intelligence (BI) team to enhance our reporting capabilities, including developing data pipelines, data warehouses, data lakes, as well as reporting and visualization tools. Key Responsibilities Engage with internal stakeholders to gain a deep understanding of business needs and provide data-driven solutions through advanced data tools, with a primary focus on Tableau for visualization and reporting. Implement and manage BI solutions to improve decision-making processes within the organization. Conduct training sessions and workshops for stakeholders to maximize the utility of Tableau dashboards and BI tools. Continuously monitor and refine BI solutions to ensure they meet the evolving needs of the business and stakeholders. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or diploma in a relevant field. Minimum of 3 years of work experience in Business Intelligence. Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience with Tableau, including dashboard development, data visualization, and storytelling with data. Experience with Snowflake and ETL tools. Proficiency in SQL. Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with a proven ability to interact effectively with internal stakeholders. Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions. Experience in the manufacturing sector is a plus. Compensation actually offered for this role will take into account internal equity and also may vary depending on the candidate's geographic region, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience among other factors. A bonus and/or long-term incentive units may be provided as part of the compensation package, in addition to the full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits, dependent on the level and position offered. Why Join IEM At IEM, you’ll join a team that powers some of the world’s most ambitious projects. We’re engineers, makers, and problem-solvers who thrive on tackling complex challenges and delivering solutions that keep industries moving forward. title: Business Intelligence Engineer
description: The Business Intelligence & AI Engineer is a key technical leader on our technology team, serving as the company’s internal Snowflake, Python, and AI data architecture expert. This role blends data engineering, advanced analytics, and AI solution design to empower data-driven decisions across the organization. Working closely with business and technology stakeholders, this position will architect, develop, and optimize data pipelines, machine learning–ready datasets, and AI-powered insights, ensuring the company gains maximum value from its data ecosystem. The role is based in Dallas, TX, and requires presence in the office at least three days per week. Key Objectives Lead the design, engineering, and optimization of Snowflake-based data platforms, including data ingestion, transformation, and governance. Develop Python-based automation, data processing, and AI workflows to scale analytics and decision support. Integrate AI and machine learning models into business intelligence solutions, leveraging APIs, predictive models, and NLP where relevant. Partner with business and technical leaders to build a modern AI-driven BI strategy that aligns with company objectives. Champion a data-as-a-service mindset — providing reliable, scalable, and secure data assets to all parts of the organization. Foster collaboration and innovation while maintaining a customer-service orientation toward internal stakeholders. Accountabilities / Core Duties Serve as a technical thought leader for Snowflake architecture, data engineering, and AI enablement. Design, build, and maintain data pipelines and ETL/ELT processes using Snowflake, dbt, Python, and orchestration tools (e.g., Airflow). Implement and manage data models supporting analytics, forecasting, and AI/ML initiatives. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to prototype and productionize AI solutions (e.g., predictive analytics, recommendation systems, or process automation). Oversee data governance, security, and compliance, ensuring all analytics and AI operations adhere to privacy and protection regulations (GDPR, CCPA, etc.). Guide and mentor team members on engineering best practices, AI integration, and scalable analytics design. Manage data performance tuning and cost optimization across Snowflake environments. Drive the continuous evolution of the BI and AI architecture roadmap, staying current on emerging technologies such as LLMs, vector databases, and data observability tools. Education & Experience Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, Information Systems, or related field. 5+ years of experience in data engineering, business intelligence, or analytics roles. Proven track record of delivering scalable Snowflake and Python-based data solutions. Experience integrating AI/ML models or automations into analytics workflows. Familiarity with modern data stacks (dbt, Airflow, Git, Docker, CI/CD) preferred. Experience in metrics-driven, growth-oriented environments (Private Equity–backed organizations ideal). Skills and Abilities Deep expertise in Snowflake and SQL, with experience designing scalable data warehouses and analytic environments. Strong Python programming skills (pandas, PySpark, scikit-learn, FastAPI, etc.) for data engineering and AI integration. Hands-on experience with AI/ML frameworks (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, OpenAI, AWS Sagemaker) a plus. Proficient in Tableau, Power BI, or equivalent visualization platforms. Strong understanding of data modeling, ETL/ELT pipelines, and data architecture principles. Excellent project management and stakeholder communication skills. Analytical mindset with the ability to translate complex data into actionable business insights. Organized, proactive, and capable of balancing multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Core Values & Leadership Competencies Servant’s Heart Empowers and develops others Leads by example with integrity and humility Communicates vision and purpose clearly Spirit of a Champion Exhibits extreme ownership and accountability Strives for excellence and constant improvement Demonstrates sound judgment and adaptability Genuine Connections Highly collaborative and empathetic communicator Builds trusted relationships across teams and departments Promotes open dialogue and inclusive problem solving

title: Senior Business Intelligence Developer
description: Important, please read: The details are below, but in plain language, we are looking for someone who specializes in and develops dashboard / data viz apps (i.e. using Sigma, Tableau, Power BI, Looker..). You should have a flare for design. We are not looking for an analytics engineer with deep ETL experience that throws together dashboards as needed. Please include a link to your portfolio showcasing apps you have developed in your resume. We cannot consider any applicants without a portfolio. About the role At Seek, Senior BI Developers go beyond crafting data-driven user experiences, they lead strategy, act as mentors, and shape the long-term vision for analytics innovation within our platform. In this advanced role, you’ll collaborate closely with data science, engineering, and product leaders to ensure scalable, best-in-class analytics solutions that empower thousands of businesses worldwide. You’ll also assume responsibility for guiding partners and developers, leveraging your deep expertise to refine analytics apps and expand the reach and value of our platform. What you'll be doing Own complex analytics apps end-to-end: Lead design and delivery of high-impact analytics applications, managing ambiguous requirements to functional solutions. Craft dashboard solutions with best-in-class design: Ensure embedded BI dashboards adhere to guiding design principles, delivering an attractive, frictionless user experience. Lead partner collaborations: Partner with customers to define requirements such as target audience, market needs, key metrics, data sources, and user workflows and orchestrate tailored, high-impact demos. Drive discovery and planning: Identify new analytics opportunities, recommend features, and influence app functionality based on usage and platform goals. Integrate and validate datasets: Ensure apps accurately leverage data models and pipelines prepared by data science. Document and communicate requirements: Clearly outline data sources, models, and business logic to align final deliverables with intended outcomes and user needs. Validate and refine solutions: Review, test, and iterate on analytics apps to ensure quality before they’re distributed on Seek’s app store. Troubleshoot and maintain analytics apps: Investigate and resolve dashboard and application issues. Mentor BI Developers: Provide guidance, feedback, and knowledge sharing to junior team members. Collaborate across teams: Work with product, engineering, and data science to ensure team deliverables align with broader platform goals and standards. Influence platform practices: Collaborate with product and engineering to recommend future tooling, process improvements, and standards. Requirements 5–7 years in BI or front-end analytics roles; proven ownership of complex analytics applications; experience in embedded dashboards or SaaS analytics platforms preferred. BI tool expertise: Deep practical experience with one or more major BI tools (e.g., Sigma Computing, Power BI, Tableau), with strong applied knowledge of visualization and dashboard design principles. SQL proficiency: Strong ability with SQL for querying, filtering, aggregations, joins, and performance‑sensitive queries. Cloud data platforms: Experience with cloud data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake, Databricks, Redshift, BigQuery) and understanding how to leverage these platforms to power analytics. Front-end analytics development: Experience building interactive dashboards or analytics features. Problem-solving & troubleshooting: Able to identify and resolve dashboard or application issues efficiently. Collaboration: Demonstrated ability to work across product, engineering, and data science teams to deliver quality analytics applications. Documentation & scalability mindset: Maintains clear documentation, templatizes processes, and automating where possible. Product delivery mindset: Track record of managing complex analytics workstreams, balancing quality, performance, and timelines. Technical leadership: Experience mentoring or guiding other developers, conducting reviews, and helping elevate team output and quality. Optional client exposure: May support internal or external stakeholders for clarifications or app demos as needed. Clear communicator: Able to present and explain complex data and analytics concepts to diverse audiences. Preferred Experience Snowflake: hands-on experience in production environments. Production analytics environments: Hands‑on experience delivering analytics solutions in a SaaS, embedded analytics, or app marketplace setting. Industry familiarity: Exposure to analytics use cases in one or more verticals (e.g., consumer goods, retail, supply chain, marketing, financial services). Process leadership: Involvement in improving team workflows, introducing reusable templates, or enhancing development standards. Broad BI ecosystem skills: Familiarity with data modeling, ETL/ELT patterns, or semantic layer concepts, even while data scientists manage heavy modeling. Advanced visualization and design skills: Experience implementing complex dashboard interactivity, advanced visual components and strong understanding of best practices and design principles. Preferably in Sigma. What Sets Us Apart Seek is committed to a culture that values ownership, flexibility, and collaboration. We (genuinely!) prioritize work-life balance and encourage our team to explore new ideas, test hypotheses, and innovate continuously. You’ll join a diverse group of professionals dedicated to pushing the boundaries of business analytics development, with ample opportunities to grow alongside talented peers who value your expertise and vision. Salary Range + Bonus: $120,000 - $150,000 Please note that we do not offer visa sponsorship for this position at this time.

This could include roles such as data analyst, data scientist, data engineer, or similar positions 7+ years of experience with SQL, ETL, data modeling, and at least one programming language (e.g., Python, C++, C#, Scala or others.) Preferred Qualifications: Master's or Ph.D degree in a STEM field About Meta: Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics. Hands-on experience with cloud platforms (AWS S3, Azure Data Lake, Google BigQuery, Snowflake). Strong knowledge of database systems (SQL, NoSQL), database design, and query optimization. Experience designing and managing data warehouses for performance and scalability. Proficiency in software engineering practices: version control (Git), CI/CD pipelines, and unit testing. title: Data Engineer (L4) - Revenue Growth
description: At Netflix, our mission is to entertain the world. Together, we are writing the next episode - pushing the boundaries of storytelling, global fandom and making the unimaginable a reality. We are a dream team obsessed with the uncomfortable excitement of discovering what happens when you merge creativity, intuition and cutting-edge technology. Come be a part of what’s next. About The Role The Membership & Finance Data Engineering team is at the heart of Netflix's financial and member data ecosystem. We build foundational datasets and data products that power critical business operations, financial reporting, and strategic analytics. As Netflix expands its business with new initiatives like Ads, Live events, and Bundles, the need for robust, scalable, and insightful revenue data has never been greater. The Revenue Growth Data Engineering team is part of the Membership and Finance Data Engineering team. It is the engine that powers insights into Netflix's financial growth. It optimizes all data related to incoming revenue streams, including subject areas such as billing, invoicing, revenue, tax, and member engagement and retention. This team’s work is foundational for financial assurance, forecasting, experimentation, and strategic decision-making, which directly impact Netflix's growth trajectory. We are seeking a Data Engineer to join our Revenue Growth Data Engineering team at Netflix. In this role, you will design, build, and maintain scalable and reliable data pipelines and data products that power insights into revenue growth and member retention analysis, while contributing to foundational datasets and metrics critical to Netflix’s financial and business reporting. Your work will directly influence how financial and growth data is produced, trusted, and consumed by teams across Finance, Product, and Analytics. You will help ensure that our data is accurate, performant, intuitive, and easy to use, enabling better decisions and deeper insights into Netflix's growth drivers. Location : Los Gatos, CA preferred, but open to Remote What You’ll Do Design, build, and maintain scalable and resilient data pipelines using Spark, Presto, and SQL, with a focus on revenue and member retention data. Develop high-quality ETL workflows to ingest, transform, and aggregate data from multiple upstream sources. Partner closely with data analysts, finance partners, and product teams to understand business needs and deliver reliable, well-modeled data products. Design and evolve data models that accurately represent financial entities, member behavior, and key growth metrics. Champion data quality and trust by implementing checks, validations, and monitoring to ensure accuracy and consistency in financial data. Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize data workflows to improve performance, reliability, and maintainability. Contribute to improving our data engineering best practices, tooling, and documentation as the platform evolves. Who We’re Looking For 3–4+ years of experience building and maintaining data pipelines in a production environment. Hands-on experience with distributed data processing technologies such as Spark and query engines like Presto. You strive to write elegant code, and you're comfortable with picking up new technologies Strong proficiency in at least one major programming language (e.g. Java, Scala, Python) and comfortable working with advanced SQL. Solid understanding of ETL design, data modeling, and data architecture fundamentals. Strong data intuition where you naturally question data, understand edge cases, and care about usability and correctness. A passion for data trustworthiness and building systems that produce accurate, consistent, and transparent financial data. Ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional partners and communicate technical concepts clearly. A mindset of ownership, curiosity, and continuous learning. Netflix provides comprehensive benefits including Health Plans, Mental Health support, a 401(k) Retirement Plan with employer match, Stock Option Program, Disability Programs, Health Savings and Flexible Spending Accounts, Family-forming benefits, and Life and Serious Injury Benefits. We also offer paid leave of absence programs. Full-time hourly employees accrue 35 days annually for paid time off to be used for vacation, holidays, and sick paid time off. Full-time salaried employees are immediately entitled to flexible time off. See more details about our Benefits here. Netflix is a unique culture and environment. Learn more here. Inclusion is a Netflix value and we strive to host a meaningful interview experience for all candidates. If you want an accommodation/adjustment for a disability or any other reason during the hiring process, please send a request to your recruiting partner. We are an equal-opportunity employer and celebrate diversity, recognizing that diversity builds stronger teams. We approach diversity and inclusion seriously and thoughtfully. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, caste, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic makeup, marital status, or military service.

title: Senior Business Intelligence Engineer
description: Tebra only initiates contact with candidates via email from an official Tebra email address (@tebra.com, @patientpop.com, or @kareo.com) or through our applicant tracking system, Greenhouse. We will only ask you to provide sensitive personal information through our official application portal — not via social media or text message. About the Role We are currently seeking a solid BI Engineer who has expertise in both back-end data processing technologies as well as building cutting edge data visualizations to provide self-service actionable analytics products. This position will play a key role in the design and development of a cloud-based Data Intelligence and Analytics services. Your Area of Focus Lead and Innovate: Help lead the design and development of scalable, efficient, and user-friendly BI solutions across the organization, ensuring alignment with business needs and objectives. Advanced Data Modeling: Develop and optimize data models, data flows, and processes to support high-quality reporting, data analysis, and business intelligence solutions. AI-Enhanced Insights: Integrate and develop AI-driven modeling, such as semantic layers, AI “cortex” frameworks, predictive analytics, and automated feature generation into BI architectures to elevate data accessibility, accelerate insight discovery, and enhance decision-making across the business. Platform Optimization: Ensure the performance and scalability of BI tools, including Tableau, Power BI, and Snowflake, by tuning data sets and providing recommendations for improvements. Stakeholder Collaboration: Engage with business leaders, product teams, and stakeholders to translate complex business requirements into technical specifications, and help define reporting and analytics needs. Complex Reporting & Dashboarding: Design, implement, and optimize complex reports and dashboards, ensuring data accuracy, consistency, and actionable insights for various business functions. Cross-functional Leadership: Work closely with data engineers and application developers to integrate and fine-tune data sources feeding BI platforms. Problem Solving & Troubleshooting: Proactively identify and resolve issues related to BI platforms, data inconsistencies, and reporting inefficiencies. Continuous Improvement: Lead efforts to improve the BI tools, processes, and standards in place. Suggest and implement best practices for data handling, visualization, and reporting. Documentation & Knowledge Sharing: Create and maintain thorough documentation for BI solutions, ensuring clear communication of processes, technical architecture, and solution designs. Mentorship & Development: Provide technical mentorship to junior team members, helping them develop their skills in BI technologies, data analysis, and reporting best practices. Thought Leadership: Contribute to strategic planning and decision-making around BI tools, data architecture, and analytics infrastructure. Your Professional Qualifications Education: BS in Computer Science, Data Science, Mathematics, or related field, or equivalent experience. A Master’s degree or relevant certifications is a plus. Experience: 5+ years of experience in Business Intelligence, data engineering, or analytics, with at least 3 years of hands-on experience with BI tools like Tableau, Power BI, and Snowflake. Advanced Technical Skills: Expertise in BI/reporting software (Power BI, Tableau), strong SQL knowledge (T-SQL, PL/SQL), and experience with big data technologies. Data Architecture & Modeling: In-depth understanding of data modeling, ETL processes, and data warehousing concepts. Ability to design data flows and pipelines for complex BI systems. Statistical & Analytical Knowledge: Proficient in data analysis, statistical techniques, and study design. Familiarity with control charts, significance testing, and experimental design. Leadership & Communication: Proven ability to lead complex projects, collaborate effectively with business and technical teams, and communicate complex data concepts to non-technical stakeholders. Problem Solving & Optimization: Strong troubleshooting skills and the ability to optimize BI solutions for performance and efficiency. Industry Knowledge: Experience working in industries with large-scale data infrastructure (Healthcare, Finance, Tech, etc.) is a plus. Certifications: Microsoft SQL Server, Oracle, Snowflake, Tableau, or Power BI certifications are highly preferred. You will develop and maintain intelligent workflows that leverage LLMs and automation frameworks to orchestrate data collection, decision-making, and actions across systems, while also building robust data pipelines and interactive interfaces where needed. Proficient with SQL and relational or NoSQL databases, plus data modeling and workflow automation. Familiar with LLM platforms and agent/tool frameworks, and able to turn sophisticated workflows into usable AI tools. Expertise in Jira, Perforce, and Git-based platforms, with the technical ability to harness APIs for data retrieval and integration. Experience with event-driven or streaming systems (e.g., Kafka, Kinesis). Expertise in dashboarding tools (e.g., PowerBI, Grafana, or EasyBI). Hands-on use of AI development tools (e.g., Cursor, Copilot). Experience leveraging AI tools or large language models (LLMs) to enhance productivity, automate workflows, or improve data-driven decision-making. NVIDIA is widely considered to be one of the world's most desirable employers in the technology field. We have some of the most forward-thinking and hardworking people in the world working for us. title: Software Engineer - Early Career (Backend/Data)
description: Summary At Apple, we believe in the power of technology to enrich people's lives. Everything we build is designed to empower people, including our advertising platform. We deliver ads in a way that benefits both customers and advertisers — helping people discover content, supporting creators, and protecting and respecting everyone’s privacy. Our technology makes advertising possible on the App Store, Apple News, Stocks, and Apple TV. We help developers and marketers of all sizes drive app discovery across the App Store. Our display ads on Apple News and Stocks let advertisers promote their products alongside trusted content from the world’s best journalists. Sponsorship integrations and experiences in live sports on Apple TV help advertisers connect with passionate fans. Everything we do is with the unwavering commitment to privacy you expect from Apple. Because when advertising is done right, it benefits everyone! Description Ads Engineering builds ad tech the Apple way—privacy-preserving, user-centric, and built to scale. Our infrastructure supports massive real-time demand with low latency, precision, and global reach. Our work spans distributed systems, large-scale data infrastructure, LLMs, and Generative AI. We design and build best of breed systems for dealing with our complex and ever-growing platform needs that help deliver highly optimized advertising content to consumers. We are excited to open applications for early-career software engineers in Cupertino (CA), Austin (TX), and New York (NY). This shared hiring process is designed to identify strong entry-level talent for our backend and data engineering teams. Candidates will complete a common interview process focused on core fundamentals and programming ability. Those who advance will then enter a team-matching phase, where they’ll be aligned with a specific team based on skills, interests, and business needs. Responsibilities Contribute to the design, implementation, and testing of backend services and data pipelines. Write clean, reliable, and efficient code in languages such as Java, Scala, or C++. Collaborate with cross functional teams including Product Management, Data, SRE, etc. to design and implement systems that can handle a variety of Ads use cases. Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Product, Data, SRE, etc.) to support features from prototype to production. Participate in code reviews, debugging, and performance tuning. Learn best practices for building systems that are scalable, secure, and low-latency. Minimum Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field (Master’s a plus). At least 1 year of relevant experience (including internships, research, or equivalent projects). Strong understanding of computer science fundamentals (data structures, algorithms, problem solving). Programming experience with Java, Scala, C++, or a similar language. Familiarity with cloud environments (AWS, GCP, or equivalent). Strong problem solving and debugging skills. Preferred Qualifications Proficiency in Java Prior internship or academic project experience with backend distributed systems or data processing (e.g., Kafka, Spark, Flink). Exposure to relational and/or non-relational databases. Knowledge of building or maintaining services in a cloud environment. Passion for learning, growth, and collaboration in a fast-paced environment. Prior experience in advertising industry is a plus Prior experience in advertising technology is a plus, but not required. We seek to promote equal opportunity for all applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, Veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics. Learn more about your EEO rights as an applicant. Apple accepts applications to this posting on an ongoing basis.

title: Data Engineer, Go-To-Market
description: About Us Notion helps you build beautiful tools for your life’s work. In today's world of endless apps and tabs, Notion provides one place for teams to get everything done, seamlessly connecting docs, notes, projects, calendar, and email—with AI built in to find answers and automate work. Millions of users, from individuals to large organizations like Toyota, Figma, and OpenAI, love Notion for its flexibility and choose it because it helps them save time and money. In-person collaboration is essential to Notion's culture. We require all team members to work from our offices on Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays, our designated Anchor Days. Certain teams or positions may require additional in-office workdays. About The Role As Notion continues to grow rapidly, we're seeking talented data engineers to join our team and help us build the foundational datasets and pipelines to support our go-to-market strategies. You'll be at the forefront of integrating our product and business systems to create rock solid processes that will propel us forward. If you're passionate about analytics use cases, data models, and solving complex data problems, then we want you on our team. What You'll Achieve You'll build core datasets to serve as the sources of truth for Notion’s marketing and sales reporting, integrating data to and from business systems data and Notion’s product. You’ll partner closely with our Marketing, Sales, Revenue Operations, Business Technology, Business Intelligence and Data Science teams to support critical reporting and analysis needs. You'll design, build and monitor pipelines that meet today's requirements but can gracefully scale with our growing data size. You’ll help democratize access to high quality data across go-to-market teams, Staff, and the entire company. Skills You'll Need To Bring You've spent 3+ years as a data engineer building core datasets and supporting business verticals as needed, ideally in product and business areas with high data volumes. You are passionate about analytics use cases, data models and solving complex data problems. You have experience working with both Marketing and Sales datasets at a SaaS company, and have built integrations with business systems like Salesforce, Netsuite, Marketo, Zendesk, etc. You are a self-starter and continuously gather and synthesize high-impact needs from business partners, design and implementing the appropriate technical solutions, and effectively communicating about deliverables, timelines and tradeoffs You have hands-on experience shipping scalable data solutions in the cloud (e.g AWS, GCP, Azure), across multiple data stores (e.g Snowflake, Redshift, Hive, SQL/NoSQL, columnar storage formats) and methodologies (e.g dimensional modeling, data marts, star/snowflake schemas) You are a SQL expert. You intimately understand aggregation functions, window functions, UDFs, self-joins, partitioning and clustering approaches to run correct and highly-performant queries You are comfortable with object-oriented programming paradigms (e.g Python, Java, Scala) You don’t need to be an AI expert, but you’re curious and willing to adopt AI tools to work smarter and deliver better results Nice To Haves You have hands-on experience in designing and building highly scalable and reliable data pipelines using BigData stack (e.g Airflow, DBT, Spark, Hive, Parquet/ORC, Protobuf/Thrift, etc) You have worked with Go-To-Market stakeholders in the past. You’ve worked at a fast-growing company or are eager to contribute in such an environment (being a current Notion user would also be great!) We hire talented and passionate people from a variety of backgrounds because we want our global employee base to represent the wide diversity of our customers. If you’re excited about a role but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every bullet point listed in the job description, we still encourage you to apply. If you’re a builder at heart, share our company values, and enthusiastic about making software toolmaking ubiquitous, we want to hear from you. Notion is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate in hiring or any employment decision based on race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, genetic information, veteran status, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or other applicable legally protected characteristic. Notion considers qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Notion is also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and disabled veterans in our job application procedures. If you need assistance or an accommodation due to a disability, please let your recruiter know. Notion is committed to providing highly competitive cash compensation, equity, and benefits. For roles based in San Francisco, the estimated base salary range for this role is $150,000 - $177,000 per year. title: Business Intelligence Engineer
description: Role Summary We’re seeking a Business Intelligence & AI Engineer to join a forward‑thinking technology team and play a critical role in shaping the company’s data and AI ecosystem. This position blends data engineering, advanced analytics, and AI solution development to create a modern, scalable decision‑support environment. In this role, you will architect Snowflake environments, build high‑quality data pipelines, and develop Python‑based analytics and AI workflows that power key business functions. You will collaborate with cross‑functional leaders to design intelligent data products, advance the company’s BI strategy, and enable meaningful data‑driven insights across the organization. Build and maintain ELT/ETL pipelines using Snowflake, dbt, Python, and orchestration tools such as Airflow. Develop machine‑learning‑ready datasets and integrate AI/ML models into business intelligence solutions. Partner with technology and business stakeholders to design and deliver AI‑enhanced analytics, predictive models, and automation tools. Oversee data governance, security, and compliance in alignment with privacy standards and regulatory frameworks. Provide technical mentorship and promote engineering best practices across the analytics and data teams. Lead performance tuning and cost optimization efforts within Snowflake environments. Contribute to the strategic roadmap for BI and AI innovation, including adoption of LLMs, vector databases, and emerging data technologies. Key Requirements 5+ years of experience in data engineering, business intelligence, or advanced analytics roles. Deep technical expertise in Snowflake, SQL, and Python (e.g., pandas, PySpark, scikit‑learn, FastAPI). Experience designing and operating scalable data warehouses, data models, and ELT/ETL frameworks. Hands‑on familiarity with AI/ML technologies, frameworks, or cloud‑based platforms (e.g., TensorFlow, PyTorch, OpenAI, SageMaker) preferred. Proficiency with modern BI tools such as Tableau or Power BI . Working knowledge of dbt , Airflow, Git, Docker, or CI/CD pipelines is beneficial. Strong analytical thinking, with the ability to translate complex data into actionable insights. Excellent communication skills, collaborative mindset, and the ability to thrive in a fast‑paced, high‑growth environment.

title: Data Engineer (L5)
description: At Netflix, our mission is to entertain the world. Together, we are writing the next episode - pushing the boundaries of storytelling, global fandom and making the unimaginable a reality. We are a dream team obsessed with the uncomfortable excitement of discovering what happens when you merge creativity, intuition and cutting-edge technology. Come be a part of what’s next. We are revolutionizing how shows and movies are produced, pushing technological boundaries to efficiently deliver streaming video at a massive scale over the internet, and continuously improving the end-to-end user experience with Netflix across their member journey. We pride ourselves on using data to inform our decision-making as we work towards our mission. This requires curating data across various domains such as Growth, Finance, Product, Content, and Studio. All of this data collection and curation is made possible thanks to the amazing Data Engineers of Netflix who bring this data to life. Data Engineering at Netflix is a role that requires building systems to process data efficiently and modeling the data to power analytics. These solutions can range from batch data pipelines that bring to life business metrics to real-time processing services that integrate with our core product features. In addition, we require our Data Engineers to have a rich understanding of large distributed systems on which our data solutions rely. Candidates should have knowledge across several of these skill sets and usually need to be deep in at least one. As a Data Engineer, you also need to have strong communication skills since you will need to collaborate with business, engineering, and data science teams to enable a culture of learning. Learn more about the work of data engineers at Netflix. Location of work: We are considering candidates who are willing to relocate to Los Gatos, California, as well as fully-remote candidates (remote in the US with occasional visits to Los Gatos) depending on the team your skills are most aligned with. Who are you? You strive to write elegant code, and you're comfortable with picking up new technologies independently You are proficient in at least one major programming language (e.g. Java, Scala, Python) and comfortable working with SQL You enjoy helping teams push the boundaries of analytical insights, creating new product features using data, and powering machine-learning models You have a strong background in at least one of the following: distributed data processing or software engineering of data services, or data modeling You are familiar with big data technologies like Spark or Flink and comfortable working with web-scale datasets You have an eye for detail, good data intuition, and a passion for data quality You appreciate the importance of great documentation and data debugging skills You relate to and embody many of the aspects of the Netflix Culture. You love working independently while also collaborating and giving/receiving candid feedback You are comfortable working in a rapidly changing environment with ambiguous requirements. You are nimble and take intelligent risks Generally, our compensation structure consists solely of an annual salary; we do not have bonuses. title: Data Engineer, BI
description: Vitamix is unquestionably the premium brand of high-performance “blenders,” and known for its top-notch company culture and climate. It is an important component of what makes Vitamix unique and successful, and the reason why Vitamix is recognized as a Top Workplace, NorthCoast99 Winner, and Smart Culture Award recipient. Throughout its 100+ year history, Vitamix has encouraged and promoted healthy lifestyles for both its customers and employees. Employees enjoy multiple wellness programs and perks, a free Vitamix machine, and an onsite fitness center. Vitamix also offers a flexible work environment, great work culture, healthy living initiatives like fitness reimbursements, and opportunities for advancement. For consumers, Vitamix can be found domestically in places like William Sonoma, Sur la Table, Crate & Barrel, Target, Amazon, and at Vitamix.com, and is in over 130 countries around the world. Ultimately, Vitamix is the Global-leader in high-performance blending solutions for home and commercial use, is preferred by chefs and is found in many of top restaurants around the world. It is relied upon by culinary schools in the U.S. and has been used to train thousands of students and master chefs worldwide. If it is not a Vitamix, it’s just a blender. Develop sophisticated dimensional models and semantic layers specifically tailored for BI reporting and analytics. Partner closely with data analysts and business stakeholders to clarify technical requirements and define Service Level Agreements (SLAs). Improve pipeline reliability and performance across assigned data domains to minimize downtime and maximize data trust. Enable stable, accurate and high-speed BI reporting through the delivery of curated datasets. Reduce data refresh times and significantly lower the frequency of operational incidents through proactive optimization. Drive the maturity of the data team by enhancing documentation and fostering a culture of knowledge sharing. Communication - Ability to express ideas clearly, confidently, and succinctly in both formal and informal settings. Flexibility - Ability to adjust quickly to changing priorities, new information, and diverse situations, while remaining focused on achieving goals. Resourcefulness - Leverages available resources and contacts to overcome obstacles and achieve project goals efficiently. title: Business Intelligence Analyst/Developer
description: Position Summary: MedReview is seeking a skilled Business Intelligence Analyst/Developer to join our Innovation & Development (PI) team. You will be instrumental in building and maintaining our BI suite, enabling data driven decision making, opportunity discovery and clear communication  focusing on key metrics using ClickHouse, SQL, and Python. Your work will directly support strategic initiatives, opportunity identification, process and quality improvement programs, and operational efficiency across the organization. Key Responsibilities: Data Engineering & Modeling: Work with the data engineer to design, develop, and maintain efficient data models, ETL/ELT processes, and data pipelines within ClickHouse for complex healthcare datasets (claims, clinical, EMR, operational). Advanced SQL & ClickHouse Development: Write complex, high-performance SQL queries and ClickHouse functions to extract, transform, and load data for reporting and analytics. Python for Data Science: Utilize Python (Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn) for data cleaning, transformation, statistical analysis, predictive modeling, and automation of BI tasks. Dashboard & Report Development: Create intuitive, interactive, and scalable dashboards and reports using BI tools ( Power BI) to visualize KPIs for clinical, financial, and operational stakeholders. Performance Improvement (PI) Focus: Translate stakeholder needs into actionable BI solutions that support quality measurement, risk stratification, care gap analysis, and operational bottlenecks. Data Quality & Governance: Ensure data accuracy, integrity, and security; implement data governance best practices. Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner with clinicians, operational leaders, IT, and other SMEs to gather requirements, provide training, and deliver data-driven insights. Ad-Hoc Analysis: Perform deep-dive analyses to answer critical business questions and support strategic decision-making. Qualifications: Experience: 3+ years in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, or a related field, with significant experience in healthcare data. Technical Skills: Expert proficiency in SQL and relational databases. Proven experience with ClickHouse (performance tuning, data modeling). Strong programming skills in Python for data manipulation and analysis. Skill in designing intuitive, professional visualizations with BI visualization tools (Tableau, Power BI, etc.). Familiarity with cloud data platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) is a plus. Healthcare Knowledge: Understanding of healthcare data standards (HL7, FHIR), claims processing, EMR data, and regulatory requirements (HIPAA). Analytical Skills: Strong problem-solving, critical thinking, and ability to translate complex data into clear narratives. Education: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Systems, Health Informatics, or a related quantitative field.

title: Data Engineer
description: This recruitment is open until filled. Early submissions are encouraged as applications will be reviewed on a regular and ongoing basis. The City of Oxnard is seeking a skilled and proactive Data Engineer to support our modernization efforts and optimize data workflows across our 300+ applications. This role will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining ETL pipelines, data transformations, and legacy systems integrations to ensure seamless data flow between city systems and third-party vendors. You will work closely with integration specialists, database administrators, and business stakeholders to enhance accessibility, reporting, and usability of critical data. This role is essential in monitoring and maintaining the data health of the city and aiding decision making through analysis and visualization. Therefore, the ideal candidate will have experience in BI and working with stakeholders to create dashboards and reports. What You’ll Do Maintain disparate datasets through ETL pipelines, improve data accessibility, ensure data integrity, and drive and enforce security compliance. Prepare strategies for modernizing legacy systems through database migrations or hybrid integrations. Collaborate with database administrators and integration specialists to streamline workflows and optimize performance. Work with system administrators to ensure data security and access control best practices. Support BI initiatives by structuring data and building dashboards for analysts. Help train and support business users to use BI tools effectively. Maintain technical documentation for data flows, integrations, and system dependencies. Analyze data trends, discrepancies, and vendor requests to guide informed decisions. Participate in evaluating and recommending software tools and platforms for data work. Payroll Title/Classification: Business Systems Analyst, Senior Work Schedule The normal work week is Monday through Friday 8:00 am-6:00 pm with every other Friday off. This position may be required to be on an on-call (stand-by) rotation and you may be required to be available to work additional hours as needed to respond to workload needs. The City does not offer hybrid or remote work. Please note: The Information Technology Department supports public safety personnel including the Police Department on a 24-hour, 7-day-per-week schedule, therefore, the candidate may be required to be on call on a rotating basis, subject to callback. As part of the selection process, applicants will be required to successfully complete a thorough background investigation, which may include a polygraph exam. This class specification represents only the core areas of responsibilities; specific position assignments will vary depending on the needs of the Department. Design, build, and maintain ETL pipelines to support data exchange between systems. Ensure data consistency, integrity, and compliance with governance regulations such as CJIS. title: Business Intelligence Developer
description: We are building our next-generation data platform at Valleywise Health, and I need a Senior BI Developer to help lay the foundation. This is a role for someone who wants to actually shape how data is used, not just deliver it. Role Highlights: You’ll partner directly with clinical and operational leaders to solve real problems, define metrics that matter, and drive decisions that directly impact patient care. Fully distributed / Remote (Health system is Phoenix-based). Technically deep and highly collaborative team. You: Strong SQL, deeply collaborative, and intentional about finding better ways. You bring the technical chops (experience with Epic data models like Clarity or Caboodle is a huge plus), but more importantly, you have the judgment and curiosity to turn messy, ambiguous data into clear operational insight. If you're ready to build an analytics framework that will be used for years to come, let's talk. Apply here: https://jobs.valleywisehealth.org/jobs/2118943-epic-business-intelligence-developer-iii or DM me if you have questions! Feel free to tag anyone in your network who might be a fit. Epic Business Intelligence Developer III Clinical Informatics Phoenix, AZ (remote, in select states) Regular FT 40 Hours Per Week - Days Req #: 48741 Summary Under the direction of the Manager IT Applications, this position has responsibility for the development, maintenance, integration, data governance and data reporting of Valleywise Health's business systems. This position analyzes existing and proposed vendor supplied software functionality for conformity with clinical health system and business workflow processes. Serves as a liaison to the clinical and business arenas, and provides subject matter expertise to translate data into information and actionable knowledge in the daily clinical or business applications practice. Utilizes specialized, proprietary software tool sets to enable reporting functionality; works within project teams to achieve team goals and outcomes; demonstrates the ability to handle multiple priorities and deadlines with independent judg ment; provides measurable customer service, and performs duties within Valleywise Health missions and values. Creates and modifies more complex reports; troubleshoots more complex level issues. T his position takes on a lead role in m entoring, developing junior analysts and educating others on best practices. In addition, this position aids in the development and maintenance of data governance standards. Based on acquired business and data knowledge, this position is able to assist end users on how to interpret data and results to drive business decisions. The successful candidate will have expertise in Epic Clarity / Caboodle and SQL. Annual Salary Range: $86,444.80 - $127,504.00 Remote Position Qualifications Education : Requires a Bachelor's degree in Business, Information Systems (IS) or related field; or an equivalent combination of training and progressively responsible experience that will result in the required specialized knowledge and abilities to perform the assigned work in lieu of degree. A Master's degree is preferred and may substitute for up to two (2) years of required experience; and a Doctorate degree may substitute for all required experience. Experience : Requires four (4) years of progressively responsible development, maintenance, integration, data governance or data reporting related experience with business systems that demonstrates a thorough understanding of the required knowledge, skills, and abilities. Experience working with Crystal Reports SQL, and other reporting tools is preferred. Certification/Licensure : If applicable to application(s) assigned to support, certification(s) for the specific business, clinical, or financial application(s)/system(s) is required and must be kept up to date. Must have a valid fingerprint clearance card issued by the Arizona Department of Public Safety OR submit a completed Affidavit at time of hire. Must complete fingerprint clearance card application within seven (7) days of hire date. Must present actual card within 90 days of applying for fingerprint clearance card. Knowledge, Skills & Abilities : Must be skilled in Microsoft Office products. Requires the ability to use complex analytical tools and methods to analyze and streamline business processes. Must be able to routinely apply working knowledge in multiple relevant disciplines through creative applications to problems/innovation. Must be able to maintain accountability for the timely resolution of specific issues and problems within a particular area, application, database, technology or system. Must demonstrate the ability to handle multiple priorities and deadlines with independent judgment. Must possess good interpersonal and communication skills both verbally and in writing including ability to inform, advise, instruct and persuade. Requires the ability to collect and analyze complex data; and perform workflow analysis and translate the outcome into definable software functionality. Requires ability to exercise considerable individual judgment and initiative in conducting the necessary facilitation and resolution of application/system design. Must be able to develop initiatives in innovative manner for effective resolution. Must be able to prepare and conduct effective presentations; assess priorities and self-time manage through deliverables; and coordinate activities with diverse groups and individuals. Requires the ability to read, write and speak effectively in English.

title: Business Intelligence Engineer II
description: Business Intelligence Engineer II – Strategic Data Team Summary We are seeking a highly skilled Business Intelligence Engineer (BIE) to drive strategically important operational and technical initiatives through data intelligence, analytics, and insights. This role is focused on building end-to-end BI solutions, improving reporting and visualization, and partnering across business and technical teams to deliver scalable, data-driven improvements. The ideal candidate has deep experience in SQL, Python, Tableau (or equivalent), large-scale datasets, and statistical analysis, with a strong ability to translate data into actionable insights for stakeholders. Key Responsibilities Architect an end-to-end BI landscape including datasets, pipelines, ETL, and data visualization to support business operations. Create new reporting for business reviews, analyzer tools, and emerging strategy efforts. Improve auditing tools to identify gaps in technology systems and enable new capabilities. Build insights-oriented visualization tools in Tableau or similar to enhance decision-making. Challenge existing processes with data-driven alternatives that improve scalability and efficiency. Partner with cross-functional teams to create and scale intelligence roadmaps that drive automation, scale, and productivity. Develop and maintain a knowledge repository for business intelligence as part of a growing center of excellence. Typical Day in the Role Assist stakeholders in managing business operations. Join meetings to discuss issues, solutions, and progress. Partner with team members to design and implement BI solutions. Write SQL queries to pull, validate, and structure data sets. Ensure deliverables are completed on time and meet expectations. Collaborate with a team of 5 members, with high interaction from day one until able to work independently. Required Skills & Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in a related field required; Master’s degree preferred. 4–6 years of strong working knowledge in SQL, Python, Tableau (or equivalent visualization tool). 2–5 years of experience working with large-scale, complex datasets. Background (academic or professional) in statistics, programming, or marketing. Strong knowledge of cloud-based data environments (e.g., AWS Redshift, Glue, Andes, or similar). Excellent communication skills, comfortable working with both technical and business stakeholders. Strong analytical mindset with the ability to decompose business requirements into actionable plans. Top 3 Must-have Skills (stack-ranked) SQL, Python, Tableau (or equivalent) – 4–6 years. Experience with large-scale datasets – 2–5 years. Background in statistics, programming, and marketing (Master’s degree preferred). Why This Role Stands Out Opportunity to impact a high-revenue subscription program supporting recurring customer engagement. Direct collaboration with stakeholders and engineers on high-visibility projects. Chance to build a strong BI portfolio through complex datasets and scalable data solutions. High-touch team environment with strong collaboration and growth opportunities. Flexibility for remote work, with potential for extension or conversion based on performance.

title: Data and Systems Analyst & BI Developer
description: The City of Saint Paul Regional Water Services is hiring an individual to support and maintain enterprise applications and data platforms, with a focus on advanced SQL, analytics, and system integrations across cloud-based and on-prem environments. This role ensures data integrity, delivers dashboards and reporting for operational and decision-making needs, resolves complex system and data issues, and contributes to modernization and continuous improvement efforts. Here's What You Can Expect To Do Lead complex systems analysis and solution design for enterprise applications and data platforms. Design, develop, and optimize advanced SQL queries, views, functions, and stored procedures to support analytics, integrations, and operational reporting. Architect and maintain ETL and data integration workflows across cloud-based and on-prem systems. Ensure enterprise data integrity, validation and reconciliation. Lead development of dashboards, analytical models, and visualizations to support executive, operational, and regulatory decision-making. Support system-to-system integrations, including REST/SOAP APIs, secure file exchanges, and database connections. Evaluate vendor solutions, participate in system selection, and support cloud migrations and modernization initiatives. Troubleshoot and resolve complex application, integration, and data issues; perform root-cause analysis and implement long-term fixes. Establish technical standards, documentation, and best practices for analytics, reporting, and data access. Develop and maintain technical documentation, SOPs, and training materials; provide advanced user and staff training. For more information and essential functions, see our job description. The ideal candidate for this position will possess the following: Experience designing Power BI semantic models and dashboards. Experience supporting cloud-based (SaaS) platforms, including data access, reporting, and integration with on-prem systems. Experience designing, writing, and maintaining REST and SOAP API calls. Scripting/programming experience (Python, JavaScript, or similar) for automation and analytics. Experience leveraging the Microsoft 365 platform (SharePoint, Power Automate, Power Apps, Excel Power Query) to automate workflows, data collection, reporting, and system integrations. Familiarity with data governance, security, and compliance in regulated environments. Experience in public sector, utilities, or critical infrastructure environments. translate business requirements into clear, efficient data visualizations Proficiency in spoken and written English $90,000 - $105,000 a year Why Trevor? title: Data Engineer, Analytics
description: Discord is used by over 200 million people every month for many different reasons, but there’s one thing that nearly everyone does on our platform: play video games. Over 90% of our users play games, spending a combined 1.5 billion hours playing thousands of unique titles on Discord each month. Discord plays a uniquely important role in the future of gaming. We are focused on making it easier and more fun for people to talk and hang out before, during, and after playing games. Discord is looking for a passionate Data Engineer to join our Data team! Data Engineers at Discord collaborate with data science and engineering teams to build and support foundational high-leverage data sets that power tools, analyses, and models. The data sets built by DE directly inform how we identify opportunities, measure success, and make decisions. We are hiring across multiple teams - including safety and growth. If this sounds exciting to you and you’re passionate about data, impact, and working on an amazing team, read on! What You Will Be Doing Create and maintain data pipelines and foundational datasets to support analytics, modeling, experimentation, and product/business needs Design and build database architectures with massive and complex data, balancing ergonomic benefits with computational load and cost Collaborate closely with data science, machine learning, and engineering teams to improve the coverage, accuracy, and reliability of instrumentation Develop audits for data quality at scale, implementing alerting and anomaly detection as necessary Partner with data scientists, engineers, and product teams to accomplish all of the above! What You Should Have 2+ years of experience building data pipelines in production environments with high volumes of consumer data 2+ years of experience in designing, developing, and maintaining robust data models from structured and unstructured sources 2+ years of experience writing accurate and effective code in SQL and Python Experience implementing and monitoring data quality with massive data sets (e.g. billions of rows) Experience improving ETL performance and cost Experience communicating clearly with your team and stakeholders, and a willingness to navigate ambiguous problems with guidance from senior teammates/management Experience collaborating directly with data science, machine learning, and product engineering teams A desire to work with amazing, passionate people who care deeply about solving challenging problems to improve Discord Last but not least - a collaborative attitude and a healthy dose of natural curiosity! Bonus Points: Passion for Discord or online communities Experience owning and proactively improving the data models for a functional area leveraging deep knowledge of performant scalable patterns Experience with modern data storage and processing technologies (i.e. BigQuery SQL, Airflow, and DBT or similar) Experience with data visualization and dashboarding technologies (i.e. Looker, Tableau, or similar) Experience with designing data sets to power a variety of use cases, including experimentation Experience working on SEO, GEO, or other top of funnel growth focused features. Experience collaborating with compliance, legal, or litigation cross-functional teams. Experience promoting and driving best practices in data governance, security, and compliance. The US base salary range for this full-time position is $160,000 to $180,000 + equity + benefits. Our salary ranges are determined by role and level. Within the range, individual pay is determined by additional factors, including job-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Please note that the compensation details listed in US role postings reflect the base salary only, and do not include equity, or benefits. Why Discord? Discord plays a uniquely important role in the future of gaming. We're a multiplatform, multigenerational and multiplayer platform that helps people deepen their friendships around games and shared interests. We believe games give us a way to have fun with our favorite people, whether listening to music together or grinding in competitive matches for diamond rank. Join us in our mission! This role collaborates across the business to identify analytic gaps, design scalable data models, and deliver high-impact dashboards and insights. You'll develop new reporting solutions, optimize existing assets, and ensure data accuracy and accessibility across internal and external stakeholders. Hands-on experience with Snowflake. Experience building data models and transformations using dbt. Strong proficiency with ThoughtSpot, Tableau, and SQL (advanced query writing required). Ability to translate business needs into scalable technical solutions. Strong communication, problem-solving, and critical thinking skills. Highly responsive, able to work under pressure, and comfortable managing multiple priorities. Experience with healthcare or pharmacy data preferred. Preferred Qualifications Expert-level ThoughtSpot development experience, including TML, Liveboards, and embedded analytics. Advanced SQL expertise, including performance tuning, complex joins, window functions, and large-scale datasets. Experience designing governed semantic layers or shared data models. Familiarity with modern data stack tools (e.g., Fivetran, Airflow, Looker, Sigma). Experience supporting external clients or working in a vendor/partner-facing environment. Background working with healthcare claims, pharmacy data, EMR/EHR datasets, or specialty pharma analytics. Experience operating in fast-growing, high-change, or startup-like environments. title: Senior Business Intelligence Developer
description: eNGINE builds Technical Teams. We are a Solutions and Placement firm shaped by decades of interaction with Technical professionals. Our inspiration is continuous learning and engagement with the markets we serve, the talent we represent, and the teams we build. Our Consulting Workforce is encouraged to enjoy career fulfillment in the form of challenging projects, schedule flexibility, and paid training/certifications. Successful outcomes start and finish with eNGINE . eNGINE is hiring a highly skilled Senior Power BI Developer to design, develop, and optimize enterprise-level business intelligence solutions. This role requires deep expertise across the full BI stack — including the data layer, semantic layer, and visualization layer — to ensure metric consistency, performance, and data integrity across all reporting platforms. The ideal candidate brings advanced Power BI development experience, strong SQL expertise, and a strong understanding of scalable data modeling and reporting architecture. Key Responsibilities: Design, develop, and maintain advanced Power BI dashboards and reports for enterprise stakeholders Develop complex DAX measures, calculated columns, and optimized data models Architect and manage the semantic layer to ensure consistent KPIs and metric definitions across the organization Partner with data engineering teams to design and optimize the data layer for reporting and analytics use cases Write and optimize complex SQL queries, views, and stored procedures Improve report performance through model optimization, query tuning, and best-practice implementation Ensure data integrity, validation, and reconciliation across reporting solutions Translate business requirements into scalable, maintainable BI solutions Establish and document BI standards, governance, and best practices Required Qualifications DAX (advanced calculations, performance tuning) Data modeling (star schema, snowflake schema, best practices) Report optimization and performance tuning Data layer architecture (warehousing concepts, ETL/ELT processes) Semantic layer design and governance Visualization best practices and storytelling with data Strong SQL experience (query optimization, complex joins, aggregations, window functions) Experience ensuring KPI alignment and metric consistency across enterprise reporting Ability to troubleshoot performance issues across the BI stack Preferred Qualifications Experience working in enterprise or highly regulated environments Familiarity with Azure, Fabric, or modern cloud data platforms Experience implementing BI governance frameworks Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders No C2C, relocation, or sponsorship for these roles. For finer details on how eNGINE can impact your career, apply today!

title: Business Intelligence Developer
description: Summary: STX Beef Co. is seeking a talented and detail-oriented Business Intelligence (BI) Developer to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for designing, developing, and maintaining BI solutions that empower data-driven decision-making across our organization. This role requires expertise in Power BI, SQL, Excel, and Azure, as well as strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Required Qualifications: · Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Information Systems, or related field, or equivalent work experience. · Proven experience developing BI solutions with Power BI, including DAX, Power Query, Dataflows, and Semantic Models. · Strong proficiency in SQL for querying, data modeling, and database management. · Advanced skills in Microsoft Excel, including formulas, VBA, and macros. · Hands-on experience with Azure data services. · Excellent analytical, troubleshooting, and communication skills. · Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment. If you are passionate about transforming data into actionable insights and have a strong background in Power BI, SQL, Excel, and Azure, we encourage you to apply for this exciting opportunity! EOE/M/F/Vet/Disabled

title: Business Intelligence (BI) Developer
description: About IEM Industrial Electric Mfg. (IEM) is the largest independent full-line manufacturer of custom power distribution systems in North America. Founded more than 75 years ago in Fremont, California, IEM grew alongside Silicon Valley and today operates over 1.7 million square feet of manufacturing capacity. With $1B+ in annual sales and 10,000+ commissioned projects across technology, data centers, commercial, energy, utilities, healthcare, industrial, and infrastructure markets, IEM continues to deliver exceptional product quality, dependable service, and the flexibility to meet complex technical requirements at scale. Business Intelligence Developer Location: Remote US Reports To: Manager of Business Intelligence Salary Range: $120,000 - $140,000 Annually Position Summary Collaborate with our newly formed Business Intelligence (BI) team to enhance our reporting capabilities, including developing data pipelines, data warehouses, data lakes, as well as reporting and visualization tools. Key Responsibilities Engage with internal stakeholders to gain a deep understanding of business needs and provide data-driven solutions through advanced data tools, with a primary focus on Tableau for visualization and reporting. Implement and manage BI solutions to improve decision-making processes within the organization. Conduct training sessions and workshops for stakeholders to maximize the utility of Tableau dashboards and BI tools. Continuously monitor and refine BI solutions to ensure they meet the evolving needs of the business and stakeholders. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or diploma in a relevant field. Minimum of 3 years of work experience in Business Intelligence. Minimum of 3 years of hands-on experience with Tableau, including dashboard development, data visualization, and storytelling with data. Experience with Snowflake and ETL tools. Proficiency in SQL. Excellent verbal and written communication skills, with a proven ability to interact effectively with internal stakeholders. Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to translate business requirements into technical solutions. Experience in the manufacturing sector is a plus. Compensation actually offered for this role will take into account internal equity and also may vary depending on the candidate's geographic region, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience among other factors. A bonus and/or long-term incentive units may be provided as part of the compensation package, in addition to the full range of medical, financial, and/or other benefits, dependent on the level and position offered. Why Join IEM At IEM, you’ll join a team that powers some of the world’s most ambitious projects. We’re engineers, makers, and problem-solvers who thrive on tackling complex challenges and delivering solutions that keep industries moving forward.
